Emne: Emne: Sundhed i Danmark med fokus på kost og motion
Praesentation:
Hej. I dag vil jeg gerne tale om sundhed i Danmark. Jeg vil især fokusere på kost og motion, fordi det betyder meget for danskernes hverdag. Det er et emne, som mange mennesker diskuterer lige nu.

Først vil jeg tale om kost. Mange danskere går meget op i at spise sundt. De officielle kostråd i Danmark siger, at vi skal spise flere grøntsager, mere fisk og mindre kød. Alligevel er det ikke alle, der følger rådene. I en travl hverdag køber mange mennesker færdigretter, som ofte indeholder for meget sukker og fedt. Desuden er fastfood blevet meget populært blandt de unge, fordi det er hurtigt og nemt. Det er et stort problem for folkesundheden.

Dernæst vil vi se på motion. Generelt er danskerne et meget aktivt folkefærd. Rigtig mange cykler til arbejde eller skole, hvilket er en god form for hverdagsmotion. Derudover er det populært at gå i fitnesscenter eller gå ture i naturen sammen med familien. Men der er også en udfordring. Mange voksne har et stillesiddende arbejde, hvor de sidder foran en computer hele dagen. Det kan føre til rygproblemer og livsstilssygdomme.

Til sidst vil jeg sige, at Danmark gør meget for sundheden, men der er stadig plads til forbedring. Det er vigtigt, at staten gør det billigere at købe sunde madvarer, så alle har råd til at leve sundt. Hvis vi gør det, kan vi få et sundere samfund i fremtiden.

Tak fordi I lyttede.
Subject: Topic: Health in Denmark with a focus on diet and exercise
Presentation:
Hello. Today I would like to talk about health in Denmark. I would especially like to focus on diet and exercise because it is very important to the everyday lives of Danes. It is a topic that many people are discussing right now.

First, I would like to talk about diet. Many Danes are very keen on eating healthy. The official dietary guidelines in Denmark say that we should eat more vegetables, more fish and less meat. However, not everyone follows the advice. In a busy everyday life, many people buy ready-made meals, which often contain too much sugar and fat. In addition, fast food has become very popular among young people because it is quick and easy. This is a big problem for public health.

Next, we will look at exercise. In general, Danes are a very active people. Many people cycle to work or school, which is a good form of everyday exercise. In addition, it is popular to go to the gym or go for walks in nature with the family. But there is also a challenge. Many adults have a sedentary job where they sit in front of a computer all day. This can lead to back problems and lifestyle diseases.

Finally, I would like to say that Denmark is doing a lot for health, but there is still room for improvement. It is important that the state makes it cheaper to buy healthy food, so that everyone can afford to live a healthy life. If we do that, we can have a healthier society in the future.

Thank you for listening.

Emne: Hvad gør du selv for at holde dig sund?
Praesentation: Jeg prøver at cykle så meget som muligt i hverdagen. Desuden forsøger jeg at spise grøntsager hver dag og drikke meget vand. Men i weekenden kan jeg godt lide at slappe af og spise lidt slik.
Subject: What do you do yourself to stay healthy?
Presentation: I try to cycle as much as possible during the day. I also try to eat vegetables every day and drink a lot of water. But on the weekend I like to relax and eat some sweets.

Emne: Tror du, det er sværere for unge mennesker at leve sundt i dag end før i tiden?
Praesentation: Ja, det tror jeg. I dag bruger unge mange timer foran en skærm, for eksempel med telefoner eller computerspil. Derfor bevæger de sig mindre. Der er også mere reklame for fastfood nu.
Subject: Do you think it is harder for young people to live healthy today than in the past?
Presentation: Yes, I think so. Today, young people spend many hours in front of a screen, for example with phones or computer games. That's why they move less. There is also more advertising for fast food now.

Emne: Hvad kan politikerne gøre for at forbedre folkesundheden?
Praesentation: Politikerne kan gøre sunde madvarer som frugt og grønt billigere ved at fjerne afgifter. De kan også lave flere cykelstier og støtte sportsklubber, så det bliver nemmere for alle at dyrke motion.
Subject: What can politicians do to improve public health?
Presentation: Politicians can make healthy foods like fruits and vegetables cheaper by removing taxes. They can also create more bike paths and support sports clubs, making it easier for everyone to exercise.

Emne: [Gratis adgang til kulturinstitutioner]Hvorfor tror du, at børn ofte får gratis adgang eller rabat til kulturinstitutioner som museer og teatre?
Praesentation: Jeg tror, at børn får gratis adgang eller rabat, fordi det er vigtigt, at de lærer om kultur og historie fra en ung alder. Hvis det er gratis eller billigt, har alle familier råd til at tage af sted, uanset hvor mange penge de har. Det hjælper også med at skabe gode vaner, så børnene fortsætter med at besøge museer og teatre, når de bliver voksne. På den måde investerer samfundet i fremtiden.
Subject: [Free admission to cultural institutions]Why do you think children often get free admission or discounts to cultural institutions like museums and theaters?
Presentation: I think children get free admission or discounts because it is important for them to learn about culture and history from a young age. If it is free or cheap, all families can afford to go, no matter how much money they have. It also helps create good habits, so the children continue to visit museums and theaters when they become adults. In that way, society is investing in the future.

Emne: [Gratis adgang til kulturinstitutioner]Synes du, det er en god idé at tage børn med på museum? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg synes, det er en rigtig god idé at tage børn med på museum. For det første lærer de meget om historie og kunst på en spændende måde, fordi de kan se tingene i virkeligheden. 

For det andet giver det familier en god oplevelse sammen, hvor man kan tale om vigtige emner. Selvom nogle børn synes, det er kedeligt i starten, kan mange museer i dag gøre det sjovt med aktiviteter for børn.
Subject: [Free admission to cultural institutions]Do you think it is a good idea to take children to museums? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think it is a very good idea to take children to museums. Firstly, they learn a lot about history and art in an exciting way, because they can see things in real life. 

Secondly, it gives families a good experience together, where you can talk about important topics. Even though some children think it is boring at first, many museums today manage to make it fun with activities for children.

Emne: [Statsstøtte til danske film]Hvorfor tror du, at kunstnere får støtte fra staten til at lave film i Danmark?
Praesentation: Jeg tror, at staten støtter danske film, fordi Danmark er et lille land med et lille sprog. Hvis der ikke var statsstøtte, ville det være meget svært for danske film at konkurrere med store Hollywood-film. Støtten sikrer, at vi kan lave film på dansk, som handler om vores egen kultur, historie og værdier. Det hjælper med at bevare den danske identitet, og det giver også unge talenter en chance for at udvikle sig.
Subject: [State subsidy for Danish films]Why do you think artists get support from the state to make films in Denmark?
Presentation: I think the state supports Danish films because Denmark is a small country with a small language. If there were no state subsidies, it would be very difficult for Danish films to compete with big Hollywood movies. The support ensures that we can make films in Danish that are about our own culture, history, and values. It helps preserve the Danish identity, and it also gives young talents a chance to develop.

Emne: [Statsstøtte til danske film]Synes du, det er vigtigt, at staten støtter danske film? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation: Ja, jeg synes, det er meget vigtigt, at staten støtter danske film. For det første hjælper det med at beskytte det danske sprog og den danske kultur, fordi vi er et lille land. Hvis staten ikke gav penge, ville de fleste mennesker nok kun se amerikanske film. For det andet giver det mulighed for at fortælle historier, som er vigtige for os, der bor i Danmark. Det behøver ikke kun at handle om at tjene penge, men om at lave god kunst.
Subject: [State subsidy for Danish films]Do you think it is important that the state supports Danish films? Why?/Why not?
Presentation: Yes, I think it is very important that the state supports Danish films. Firstly, it helps protect the Danish language and Danish culture because we are a small country. If the state did not give money, most people would probably only watch American films. Secondly, it makes it possible to tell stories that are important to those of us who live in Denmark. It does not only have to be about making money, but about making good art.

Emne: Nogle kommuner vælger at bruge mange penge på store, internationale sportsbegivenheder. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det for kommunerne?
Praesentation: En stor fordel er, at det skaber liv i byen og tiltrækker mange turister, som bruger penge på hoteller og restauranter. Det kan også gøre kommunen kendt i udlandet og give borgerne en god fælles oplevelse. En ulempe er dog, at det er utroligt dyrt. Hvis kommunen bruger for mange penge på sport, kan der mangle penge til andre vigtige ting, som for eksempel folkeskoler, ældrepleje eller lokal kultur.
Subject: Some municipalities choose to spend a lot of money on large, international sports events. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be for the municipalities?
Presentation: A big advantage is that it creates life in the city and attracts many tourists who spend money on hotels and restaurants. It can also make the municipality well-known abroad and give the citizens a great shared experience. However, a disadvantage is that it is incredibly expensive. If the municipality spends too much money on sports, there might be a lack of money for other important things, such as public schools, elderly care, or local culture.

Emne: Synes du, at kommuner bør prioritere andre områder som børnepasning og ældrepleje frem for store sportsbegivenheder? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation: Ja, det synes jeg. Efter min mening er det vigtigere at bruge penge på børnepasning og ældrepleje, fordi det handler om borgernes basale velfærd og hverdag. Hvis de ældre ikke får en god pleje, eller hvis der mangler pædagoger i vuggestuerne, rammer det mange familier hårdt. Store sportsbegivenheder er sjove og spændende, men de varer kun i kort tid. Velfærd er noget, vi har brug for hver evig eneste dag.
Subject: Do you think that municipalities should prioritize other areas like childcare and elderly care over large sports events? Why?/Why not?
Presentation: Yes, I think so. In my opinion, it is more important to spend money on childcare and elderly care because it is about the citizens' basic welfare and everyday lives. If the elderly do not get good care, or if there is a shortage of educators in nurseries, it hits many families hard. Large sports events are fun and exciting, but they only last for a short time. Welfare is something we need each and every day.

Emne: [Uro i Klassen]Hvorfor tror du, der er meget uro i nogle folkeskoleklasser?
Praesentation: Jeg tror, der er meget uro, fordi klasserne ofte er meget store, og eleverne er meget forskellige. Det kan være svært for én lærer at hjælpe alle på samme tid. Hvis nogle elever keder sig, eller hvis opgaverne er for svære, begynder de måses at larme eller snakke med sidemanden. Desuden bruger mange børn meget tid foran en skærm i dag, og det kan gøre det sværere for dem at sidde stille og koncentrere sig i skolen.
Subject: [Noise in the Classroom]Why do you think there is a lot of noise and unrest in some public school classrooms?
Presentation: I think there is a lot of noise because classes are often very large, and the students are very different. It can be difficult for one teacher to help everyone at the same time. If some students are bored, or if the assignments are too difficult, they might start making noise or talking to the person next to them. Furthermore, many children spend a lot of time in front of a screen nowadays, and that can make it harder for them to sit still and concentrate in school.

Emne: [Uro i Klassen]Hvad mener du, lærerne kan gøre for at begrænse uroen i klasserne?
Praesentation: Jeg mener, at lærerne kan gøre flere ting. For det første er det vigtigt at have klare regler for, hvordan man opfører sig i klassen, så alle elever ved, hvad de skal gøre. For det andet kan lærerne lave mere varieret undervisning, hvor eleverne ikke kun skal sidde stille, men også bevæge sig eller arbejde i grupper. Endelig er det en god idé at opbygge et godt forhold til eleverne, fordi det skaber mere respekt og ro.
Subject: [Noise in the Classroom]What do you think teachers can do to limit the noise and unrest in classrooms?
Presentation: I think that teachers can do several things. Firstly, it is important to have clear rules for how to behave in class, so all students know what to do. Secondly, teachers can create more varied teaching, where students do not just have to sit still, but also move around or work in groups. Finally, it is a good idea to build a good relationship with the students, because it creates more respect and quiet.

Emne: [Forskellige Undervisningsmetoder]Hvorfor tror du, at nogle lærere vælger kreative undervisningsmetoder som gruppearbejde og projekter?
Praesentation: Jeg tror, at lærere bruger kreative metoder, fordi det gør undervisningen mere spændende for eleverne. Når man arbejder i grupper eller med projekter, lærer børnene at samarbejde og løse problemer sammen. Det giver også plads til, at eleverne kan være aktive i stedet for bare at lytte til læreren i lang tid. På den måde husker de ofte det, de har lært, meget bedre.
Subject: [Different Teaching Methods]Why do you think some teachers choose creative teaching methods like group work and projects?
Presentation: I think teachers use creative methods because it makes teaching more exciting for the students. When working in groups or with projects, children learn to cooperate and solve problems together. It also allows the students to be active instead of just listening to the teacher for a long time. In that way, they often remember what they have learned much better.

Emne: [Forskellige Undervisningsmetoder]Mener du, det er vigtigt at bruge forskellige undervisningsmetoder? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation: Ja, jeg mener, det er meget vigtigt at bruge forskellige undervisningsmetoder. Børn lærer på forskellige måder; nogle lærer bedst ved at læse, mens andre lærer bedst ved at lytte eller lave noget praktisk. Hvis læreren varierer undervisningen, kan alle elever være med og forstå stoffet. Det gør også skoledagen mere spændende, så eleverne ikke mister koncentrationen og keder sig.
Subject: [Different Teaching Methods]Do you think it is important to use different teaching methods? Why?/Why not?
Presentation: Yes, I think it is very important to use different teaching methods. Children learn in different ways; some learn best by reading, while others learn best by listening or doing something practical. If the teacher varies the teaching, all students can participate and understand the material. It also makes the school day more exciting, so the students do not lose concentration and get bored.

Emne: Nogle mener, at der bør bruges flere penge på folkeskolen for at ansætte flere lærere. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
En stor fordel er, at der bliver flere voksne til at hjælpe eleverne. Når der er to lærere i en klasse, eller hvis klasserne bliver mindre, får den enkelte elev mere opmærksomhed. Det kan give mere ro og bedre indlæring. 

En ulempe er dog, at det koster rigtig mange penge for samfundet. Hvis man bruger flere penge på skolerne, skal man måske spare på andre vigtige områder som sundhedsvæsenet eller ældreplejen.
Subject: Some believe that more money should be spent on the public school system to hire more teachers. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there could be to that?
Presentation:
A big advantage is that there will be more adults to help the students. When there are two teachers in a class, or if classes become smaller, each individual student gets more attention. This can result in more quiet and better learning. 

However, a disadvantage is that it costs a lot of money for society. If more money is spent on schools, it might be necessary to save on other important areas like the healthcare system or elderly care.

Emne: Synes du, at penge brugt på folkeskolen er en god investering? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation: Ja, jeg synes, at penge brugt på folkeskolen er en rigtig god investering. Børnene er jo vores fremtid, og det er vigtigt, at de får en god uddannelse fra starten. Hvis skolerne har gode lærere og materialer, lærer børnene mere, og de klarer sig bedre senere i livet. Det betyder, at de kan få gode job og bidrage til samfundet, når de bliver voksne. Derfor betaler pengene sig tilbage i sidste ende.
Subject: Do you think that money spent on the public school system is a good investment? Why?/Why not?
Presentation: Yes, I think that money spent on public schools is a very good investment. After all, children are our future, and it is important that they get a good education from the start. If schools have good teachers and materials, children learn more, and they do better later in life. This means they can get good jobs and contribute to society when they become adults. Therefore, the money pays itself back in the end.

Emne: [Mangel på arbejdskraft i sundhedssektoren]Hvorfor tror du, der mangler arbejdskraft i sundhedssektoren, fx sygeplejersker og social- og sundhedsassistenter?
Praesentation: Jeg tror, der mangler arbejdskraft, fordi arbejdet i sundhedssektoren ofte er meget hårdt og stressende. Sygeplejersker og assistenter skal løbe stærkt, og de har mange skæve arbejdstider, som for eksempel nattevagter og weekendarbejde. Desuden synes mange, at lønnen er for lav i forhold til det store ansvar, de har. Det gør, at færre unge vælger uddannelsen, og nogle forlader faget for at finde et andet job.
Subject: [Labor shortage in the healthcare sector]Why do you think there is a shortage of labor in the healthcare sector, for example, nurses and social and healthcare assistants?
Presentation: I think there is a shortage of labor because work in the healthcare sector is often very hard and stressful. Nurses and assistants have to run fast, and they have many awkward working hours, such as night shifts and weekend work. Furthermore, many people think that the salary is too low compared to the big responsibility they have. This means that fewer young people choose the education, and some leave the profession to find another job.

Emne: [Klima og livsstil / At vælge en klimavenlig livsstil]Hvorfor tror du, at nogle vælger at leve klimavenligt, fx ved at spise mindre kød eller tage offentlig transport?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at mange vælger at leve klimavenligt, fordi de er bekymrede for fremtiden og klimaforandringerne. De vil gerne gøre en personlig forskel for at passe på planeten og mindske CO2-udledningen. 

Derudover er det blevet meget moderne at leve grønt, og der er kommet mange gode alternativer, som for eksempel lækker vegetarmad og bedre offentlig transport. Nogle gør det også for at spare penge eller for at leve sundere.
Subject: [Climate and lifestyle / Choosing a climate-friendly lifestyle]Why do you think some people choose to live in a climate-friendly way, for example by eating less meat or taking public transportation?
Presentation:
I think many people choose to live in a climate-friendly way because they are worried about the future and climate change. They want to make a personal difference to take care of the planet and reduce CO2 emissions. 

In addition, living green has become very fashionable, and many good alternatives have emerged, such as delicious vegetarian food and better public transportation. Some also do it to save money or to live healthier.

Emne: [Klima og livsstil / At vælge en klimavenlig livsstil]Hvad tror du, det betyder for miljøet, hvis flere lever klimavenligt?
[Klima og livsstil / At ignorere klimaudfordringer]Hvorfor tror du, at nogle mennesker ikke ændrer deres livsstil, selvom de ved, at klimaet er under pres?
[Klima og livsstil / At ignorere klimaudfordringer]Hvordan kan samfundet opmuntre folk til at tage mere hensyn til klimaet?
[Klima og livsstil / At ignorere klimaudfordringer]Nogle mener, at vi skal betale mere i skat for at finansiere klimainitiativer. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
[Mangel på arbejdskraft i sundhedssektoren]Hvilke problemer mener du, der kan være ved, at der mangler sygeplejersker og social og sundhedsassistenter?
[Mangel på arbejdskraft i byggebranchen]Hvorfor tror du, der mangler arbejdskraft i byggebranchen, fx tømrere og betonarbejdere?
[Mangel på arbejdskraft i byggebranchen]Hvilke problemer mener du, der kan være ved, at der mangler arbejdskraft inden for byggebranchen?
Ved mangel på arbejdskraft kan man gøre det nemmere at rekruttere udenlandsk arbejdskraft. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det for samfundet?
Mener du, det er i orden at rekruttere udenlandsk arbejdskraft, selvom der er arbejdsløse i Danmark? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
[At søge læge ved små problemer]Hvorfor tror du, at nogle vælger at gå til lægen, selvom deres problemer ikke er alvorlige?
[At søge læge ved små problemer]Kan der være nogen problemer ved, at folk går til lægen for små problemer?
[At undgå lægebesøg]Hvorfor tror du, at nogle undgår at gå til lægen, selvom de har helbredsproblemer?
[At undgå lægebesøg]Hvad kan det betyde for en persons helbred, hvis de undgår at gå til lægen?
I nogle lande betaler man for at gå til lægen. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Synes du, at lægehjælp skal være gratis for alle? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
[A-obl. Mænd venter med at gå til læge]Der er en tendens til,  at mænd venter længere end kvinder med at gå til læge, selvom de ikke føler sig helt raske. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er flere grunde til, at mænd venter med at gå til læge. 

For det første vil mange mænd gerne virke stærke. De tænker tit, at problemet går væk af sig selv, og de vil ikke virke svage eller syge over for deres familie eller kolleger. 

For det andet har mange mænd travlt med deres arbejde, så de prioriterer ikke deres helbred i hverdagen. 

Endelig kan det også handle om frygt. Nogle mænd frygter, at lægen finder noget alvorligt, og derfor undgår de at bestille en tid. Det er selvfølgelig ikke godt, fordi det er vigtigt at opdage sygdomme i god tid.
Subject: [Men wait to see a doctor]There is a tendency for men to wait longer than women to see a doctor, even if they don't feel completely well. Why do you think this is the case?
Presentation:
I think there are several reasons why men wait to go to the doctor. 

Firstly, many men want to seem strong. They often think that the problem will go away by itself, and they do not want to seem weak or sick to their family or colleagues. 

Secondly, many men are busy with their work, so they do not prioritize their health in their daily lives. 

Finally, it can also be about fear. Some men fear that the doctor will find something serious, and therefore they avoid booking an appointment. This is of course not good, because it is important to detect illnesses early.

Emne: [A-opf. Mænd venter med at gå til læge]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved, at man venter lidt med at gå til læge, selvom man ikke føler sig helt rask?
Praesentation:
Ja, det mener jeg bestemt. 

Hvis man venter for længe, kan en lille sygdom blive meget værre og sværere at behandle. For eksempel kan alvorlige sygdomme som kræft eller hjerteproblemer opdages for sent. 

Det kan også betyde, at man skal være sygemeldt fra sit arbejde i længere tid, hvilket påvirker både en selv og samfundet. Så det er altid bedst at reagere i tide.
Subject: [Men wait to see a doctor]Do you think there might be any problems with waiting a little to see a doctor, even if you don't feel completely well?
Presentation:
Yes, I definitely think so. 

If you wait too long, a small illness can become much worse and harder to treat. For example, serious illnesses like cancer or heart problems might be discovered too late. 

It can also mean that you have to be on sick leave from your job for a longer time, which affects both yourself and society. So it is always best to react in time.

Emne: [B-obj. Videokonsultation med lægen]Flere og flere bruger videokonsultation i stedet for at møde fysisk op hos lægen. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er flere grunde til, at videokonsultationer er populære. 

For det første er det meget nemt og sparer tid. Man behøver ikke at køre til lægen eller sidde i venteværelset, hvilket er en stor fordel, hvis man har en travl hverdag eller har små børn. 

For det andet er det smart, hvis man er meget syg eller har svært ved at gå. Så kan man tale med lægen direkte fra sin sofa. 

Det er dog vigtigt at huske, at videokonsultation ikke kan bruges til alt. Nogle gange er det nødvendigt, at lægen undersøger en fysisk.
Subject: [Video consultation with the doctor] More and more people are using video consultation instead of physically meeting the doctor. Why do you think this is so?
Presentation:
I think there are several reasons why video consultations are popular. 

Firstly, it is very easy and saves time. You do not have to drive to the doctor or sit in the waiting room, which is a great advantage if you have a busy daily life or have small children. 

Secondly, it is smart if you are very sick or have difficulty walking. Then you can talk to the doctor directly from your couch. 

However, it is important to remember that video consultation cannot be used for everything. Sometimes it is necessary for the doctor to examine you physically.

Emne: [B-opf. Videokonsultation med lægen]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved at bruge videokonsultation i stedet for at møde fysisk op hos lægen? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, det tror jeg, der kan være. 

Det største problem er, at lægen ikke kan undersøge din krop rigtigt. Lægen kan for eksempel ikke lytte til dit hjerte eller tage en blodprøve over en skærm. Hvis man er meget syg, er det ikke sikkert, at en video giver det rigtige billede af situationen. 

Derudover kan der være tekniske problemer med internettet, og nogle ældre mennesker synes måske, det er svært at bruge teknologien.
Subject: [Video consultation with the doctor] Do you think there can be any problems with using video consultation instead of showing up physically at the doctor's office? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be. 

The biggest problem is that the doctor cannot examine your body properly. For example, the doctor cannot listen to your heart or take a blood sample through a screen. If you are very sick, it is not certain that a video gives the right picture of the situation. 

In addition, there can be technical problems with the internet, and some elderly people might find it difficult to use the technology.

Emne: [Obj.]Alle har ret til gratis behandling på et hospital, men man har ofte mulighed for at blive behandlet hurtigere på et privathospital, hvis man selv kan og vil betale for det. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det for samfundet?
Praesentation:
På den ene side kan en fordel være, at ventelisterne på de offentlige hospitaler bliver kortere. Når nogle vælger at betale for en hurtig operation på et privathospital, tager de mindre plads i det offentlige system. Det betyder, at andre kan komme hurtigere til.

På den anden side er der en stor ulempe, og det handler om ulighed. Hvis rige mennesker kan købe sig til en hurtigere behandling, får vi et samfund, hvor sundhed afhænger af penge. Det er ikke fair, fordi alle burde have de samme muligheder, når de er syge. Desuden kan privathospitalerne tiltrække de dygtigste læger og sygeplejersker fra det offentlige system, fordi de måske kan tilbyde en højere løn eller bedre arbejdstider. Det kan gøre det offentlige sygehusvæsen svagere.
Subject: Everyone has the right to free treatment at a hospital, but you often have the opportunity to be treated faster at a private hospital if you can and want to pay for it yourself. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there could be for society?
Presentation:
On one hand, an advantage can be that the waiting lists at public hospitals become shorter. When some people choose to pay for a quick surgery at a private hospital, they take up less space in the public system. This means that others can get their treatment faster.

On the other hand, there is a major disadvantage, and it is about inequality. If wealthy people can buy their way to faster treatment, we get a society where health depends on money. That is not fair, because everyone should have the same opportunities when they are sick. Furthermore, private hospitals can attract the most skilled doctors and nurses away from the public system because they might offer higher salaries or better working hours. This can make the public healthcare system weaker.

Emne: [Obf.]Mener du, det er rimeligt, at folk kan blive behandlet hurtigere, hvis de kan og vil betale for det? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Det er et svært spørgsmål, men personligt synes jeg ikke, det er helt rimeligt. I et land som Danmark skal sundhed være ens for alle, uanset hvor mange penge man har på sin bankkonto. Hvis rige mennesker kan betale sig fra at stå i kø, skaber det en unødvendig ulighed i samfundet. Det kan føre til, at vi får et "A-hold" og et "B-hold" inden for sundhed, og det synes jeg er forkert, for sygdom rammer jo alle lige.

Når det er sagt, kan jeg godt forstå, at folk gør det, hvis de har ondt og gerne vil hurtigt tilbage på arbejde. Men principielt mener jeg, at det offentlige sygehusvæsen skal være så stærkt, at ingen behøver at betale ekstra for at få hjælp i ordentlig tid.
Subject: Do you think it is fair that people can get treated faster if they can and want to pay for it? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
It is a difficult question, but personally, I do not think it is entirely fair. In a country like Denmark, healthcare should be the same for everyone, regardless of how much money they have in their bank account. If wealthy people can pay to skip the line, it creates an unnecessary inequality in society. This can lead to us having an "A-team" and a "B-team" within healthcare, and I think that is wrong because illness affects everyone equally.

That being said, I can easily understand why people do it if they are in pain and want to get back to work quickly. However, in principle, I believe that the public healthcare system should be so strong that no one needs to pay extra to get help in a reasonable amount of time.

Emne: [A-obl. Klima og madvaner]Antallet af vegetarer og veganere er steget de senere år. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to store grunde til det. For det første tænker mange mennesker på klimaet og miljøet. De ved, at kødproduktion udleder meget CO2, så de vil gerne hjælpe planeten ved at spise mindre kød. 

For det andet går folk meget op i deres sundhed i dag. Mange føler sig mere friske og sunde, når de spiser flere grøntsager. Desuden er det blevet meget nemmere at finde vegetarmad i supermarkederne nu.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Climate and food habits] The number of vegetarians and vegans has increased in recent years. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two big reasons for that. Firstly, many people think about the climate and the environment. They know that meat production emits a lot of CO2, so they want to help the planet by eating less meat. 

Secondly, people care a lot about their health nowadays. Many feel more fresh and healthy when they eat more vegetables. Furthermore, it has become much easier to find vegetarian food in the supermarkets now.

Emne: [A-Opf. Klima og madvaner]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved at være vegetar eller veganer? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, der kan være et par problemer. For det første kan det være svært at få alle de rigtige vitaminer, for eksempel B12-vitamin, som man normalt får fra kød. Man skal passe meget på og måske tage kosttilskud. 

For det andet kan det være svært i sociale situationer. Hvis man bliver inviteret til middag hos venner eller familie, skal de lave speciel mad, og det kan være lidt besværligt for dem. Men hvis man planlægger det godt, kan man sagtens klare det.
Subject: [A-Follow-up. Climate and food habits] Do you think there can be any problems with being a vegetarian or vegan? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be a few problems. Firstly, it can be difficult to get all the right vitamins, for example vitamin B12, which you normally get from meat. You have to be very careful and perhaps take supplements. 

Secondly, it can be difficult in social situations. If you are invited to dinner at friends' or family's houses, they have to make special food, and that can be a bit of a hassle for them. However, if you plan it well, you can easily manage it.

Emne: [A-obl. Kvinder i traditttionelle mandejob]I Danmark er der færre kvinder end mænd, der arbejder med IT og finans. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om traditioner og gamle vaner. Lige fra de er små, lærer drenge tit at lege med computere og tal, mens piger vælger andre ting. Det gør, at mange kvinder ikke tænker på IT eller finans som en mulighed, når de bliver voksne. 

For det andet tror jeg, at arbejdskulturen i disse brancher kan virke hård. Mange tror, at man skal arbejde meget sent om aftenen, og det kan være svært at kombinere med at have små børn og en familie derhjemme.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Women in traditional men's jobs] In Denmark, there are fewer women than men working in IT and finance. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about traditions and old habits. Right from when they are small, boys are often taught to play with computers and numbers, while girls choose other things. This means that many women do not think of IT or finance as an option when they grow up. 

Secondly, I think that the work culture in these industries can seem tough. Many people believe that you have to work very late in the evening, and that can be difficult to combine with having young children and a family at home.

Emne: [A-opf. Kvinder i traditttionelle mandejob]Kan der efter din mening være nogen problemer ved, at der er færre kvinder end mænd, der arbejder med IT og finans? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, det kan skabe problemer. 

For det første mangler IT- og finansbranchen mange dygtige medarbejdere. Hvis virksomhederne næsten kun ansætter mænd, går de glip af mange kloge og kreative kvinder, som kunne lave gode produkter. 

For det andet handler det om ligestilling og økonomi. Job inden for IT og finans har ofte meget høje lønninger. Når der ikke er så mange kvinder i de job, kommer mænd i gennemsnit til at tjene flere penge end kvinder, og det skaber ulighed i samfundet.
Subject: [A-follow-up. Women in traditional men's jobs] In your opinion, can there be any problems with the fact that there are fewer women than men working in IT and finance? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think it can create problems. 

Firstly, the IT and finance sectors lack many skilled employees. If companies almost exclusively hire men, they miss out on many smart and creative women who could make good products. 

Secondly, it is about equality and economics. Jobs within IT and finance often have very high salaries. When there are not as many women in those jobs, men will on average earn more money than women, and that creates inequality in society.

Emne: [A-obl. I dagsinstitution fra en tidlig alder]Det er almindeligt i Danmark, at børn kommer i daginstitution, når de er cirka et år gamle. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?]
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første skal de fleste forældre i Danmark tilbage på arbejde efter et års barsel. Både mænd og kvinder arbejder meget, og derfor har de brug for, at nogen passer deres børn. 

For det andet tror jeg, det er godt for børnene. I vuggestuen lærer de at lege med andre børn og være sociale fra en tidlig alder. Det hjælper dem med at blive selvstændige. 

Så det er både praktisk for forældrene og godt for barnets udvikling.
Subject: [A-obl. In daycare from an early age] It is common in Denmark for children to start daycare when they are about one year old. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for this. 

Firstly, most parents in Denmark have to go back to work after one year of parental leave. Both men and women work a lot, and therefore they need someone to look after their children. 

Secondly, I think it is good for the children. In daycare, they learn to play with other children and be social from an early age. It helps them become independent. 

So it is both practical for the parents and good for the child's development.

Emne: [A-opf. I dagsinstitution fra en tidlig alder]Hvad tror du, det betyder for børnenes sociale udvikling, at de kommer tidligt i daginstitution?]
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det betyder meget for børnenes sociale udvikling. 

For det første lærer børnene at være sammen med andre børn i en tidlig alder. De lærer at dele legetøj, at samarbejde og at tage hensyn til hinanden, hvilket er meget vigtigt for deres fremtid. 

For det andet møder de voksne, som ikke er deres forældre, og det gør dem mere selvstændige. 

På den anden side kan det også være svært for nogle børn, hvis de er meget små og har brug for meget ro. 

Men generelt tror jeg, det er en fordel, fordi det forbereder dem godt til at starte i skole.
Subject: [A-opf. In daycare from an early age] What do you think it means for children's social development that they start daycare early?
Presentation:
I think it means a lot for children's social development. 

Firstly, children learn to be with other children at an early age. They learn to share toys, to cooperate, and to show consideration for each other, which is very important for their future. 

Secondly, they meet adults who are not their parents, and that makes them more independent. 

On the other hand, it can also be difficult for some children if they are very small and need a lot of quiet time. 

But generally, I think it is an advantage because it prepares them well for starting school.

Emne: [B-obl. At blive passet af mor eller far]Der er nogle forældre, der vælger at passe deres børn selv, selvom der er offentlige daginstitutioner. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er flere grunde til, at nogle forældre vælger at passe deres børn selv. 

For det første vil de gerne tilbringe mere tid sammen med deres børn, når de er helt små. De føler, at de første år er meget vigtige for barnets tryghed. 

For det andet vil de gerne undgå, at hverdagen bliver for stresset med tidlige morgener og afleveringer. 

På den anden side går barnet glip af at være sammen med andre børn hver dag. 

Men for mange familier giver det bare mere ro at være hjemme i starten.
Subject: [B-obl. Being cared for by mom or dad] There are some parents who choose to look after their children themselves, even though there are public daycare institutions. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are several reasons why some parents choose to take care of their children themselves. 

Firstly, they want to spend more time with their children when they are very small. They feel that the first years are very important for the child's sense of security. 

Secondly, they want to avoid daily life becoming too stressful with early mornings and drop-offs. 

On the other hand, the child misses out on being with other children every day. 

But for many families, staying at home in the beginning simply provides more peace of mind.

Emne: [B-opf. At blive passet af mor eller far]Kan der være nogen problemer ved, at børn ikke kommer i daginstitution, før de skal begynde i skolen? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, der kan være nogle problemer. 

For det første lærer børnene ikke at være en del af en stor gruppe, hvis de kun er derhjemme. De kan mangle erfaring med at dele legetøj, løse konflikter og samarbejde med andre børn på deres egen alder. 

For det andet kan det blive et stort chok for dem at starte i skole, fordi de pludselig skal sidde stille, høre efter læreren og klare sig uden deres forældre en hel dag. Det kan gøre skolestarten meget sværere og mere utryg for barnet.
Subject: [B-opf. Being cared for by mom or dad] Can there be any problems if children do not go to daycare before they start school? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be some problems. 

Firstly, children do not learn to be part of a large group if they are only at home. They may lack experience with sharing toys, resolving conflicts, and cooperating with other children their own age. 

Secondly, starting school can be a big shock for them because they suddenly have to sit still, listen to the teacher, and manage without their parents for a whole day. This can make the transition to school much harder and more insecure for the child.

Emne: [A-obl. Cyklen som transportmiddel]I Danmark er der mange, der bruger cyklen til transport, fx til og fra arbejde. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første er Danmark et meget fladt land, og byerne er bygget til cyklister. Der er gode cykelstier overalt, og det gør det nemt og sikkert at cykle. 

For det andet handler det om sundhed og økonomi. Mange danskere kan godt lide at røre sig i hverdagen, og det er meget billigere at cykle end at køre i bil eller købe billetter til bussen og toget. Det er også godt for miljøet.
Subject: [A-compulsory. The bicycle as a means of transportation] In Denmark, there are many people who use the bicycle for transportation, for example, to and from work. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, Denmark is a very flat country, and the cities are built for cyclists. There are good bike lanes everywhere, and that makes it easy and safe to bike. 

Secondly, it is about health and economy. Many Danes like to exercise in their daily lives, and it is much cheaper to bike than to drive a car or buy tickets for the bus and the train. It is also good for the environment.

Emne: [A-opf. Cyklen som transportmiddel]Hvad tror du, det betyder for trafiksikkerheden i byerne, at der er mange, der bruger cyklen til transport?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan betyde to forskellige ting. 

På den ene side er det godt for trafiksikkerheden, fordi bilerne ofte kører langsommere i byerne, når der er mange cyklister. Det kan give færre alvorlige ulykker. 

På den anden side kan det også skabe problemer og stress i trafikken. Hvis der er rigtig mange cykler, biler og busser på vejen på samme tid, kan det være svært at holde øje med alle, og så kan der nemt ske små uheld i lyskrydsene.
Subject: [A-follow-up. The bicycle as a means of transportation] What do you think it means for traffic safety in the cities that there are many people who use the bicycle for transportation?
Presentation:
I think it can mean two different things. 

On one hand, it is good for traffic safety because cars often drive slower in the cities when there are many cyclists. This can lead to fewer serious accidents. 

On the other hand, it can also create problems and stress in traffic. If there are a great many bicycles, cars, and buses on the road at the same time, it can be difficult to keep an eye on everyone, and then minor accidents can easily happen at the intersections.

Emne: [B-obl. Bilen son transportmiddel]Der er mange, der vælger bilen til transport, fx til og fra arbejde, selvom der i Danmark er gode muligheder for offentlig transport. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om fleksibilitet og tid. Hvis man tager bilen, kan man køre lige, når man vil, og man behøver ikke at vente på bussen eller toget. Det sparer meget tid i en travl hverdag. 

For det andet handler det om komfort. I sin egen bil har man altid et siddeplads, man kan høre sin egen musik, og det er nemmere, hvis man skal købe ind eller hente børn på vejen hjem.
Subject: [B-compulsory. The car as a means of transportation] There are many people who choose the car for transportation, for example, to and from work, even though there are good options for public transportation in Denmark. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about flexibility and time. If you take the car, you can drive exactly when you want, and you do not have to wait for the bus or the train. This saves a lot of time in a busy daily life. 

Secondly, it is about comfort. In your own car, you always have a seat, you can listen to your own music, and it is easier if you need to go grocery shopping or pick up children on the way home.

Emne: [B-opf. Bilen son transportmiddel]Kan der efter din mening være nogen problemer ved, at der er mange, der vælger bilen til transport? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, det kan skabe store problemer. 

For det første er det skidt for miljøet og klimaet, fordi biler forurener meget og udleder meget CO2. 

For det andet skaber de mange biler lange køer på vejene, især om morgenen og om eftermiddagen. Det betyder, at folk spilder meget tid i trafikken, og det kan gøre folk stressede i deres hverdag.
Subject: [B-follow-up. The car as a means of transportation] In your opinion, can there be any problems with the fact that there are many people who choose the car for transportation? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think it can create big problems. 

Firstly, it is bad for the environment and the climate because cars pollute a lot and emit a lot of CO2. 

Secondly, the many cars create long queues on the roads, especially in the morning and in the afternoon. This means that people waste a lot of time in traffic, and that can make people stressed in their daily lives.

Emne: [A-obl. Uddannelser, der kræver høje karakterer]På mange uddannelser er der en begrænsning på, hvor mange studerende der bliver optaget. Hvorfor tror du, der er det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om plads og ressourcer på universiteterne. Skolerne har ikke nok lokaler, computere eller undervisere, hvis der kommer alt for mange studerende på én gang. 

For det andet handler det om jobmarkedet efter uddannelsen. Hvis samfundet ikke mangler for eksempel journalister eller psykologer, er det en dårlig idé at uddanne for mange, fordi de så vil blive arbejdsløse.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Educations that require high grades] On many study programs, there is a limit on how many students are admitted. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about space and resources at the universities. The schools do not have enough classrooms, computers, or teachers if too many students arrive at the same time. 

Secondly, it is about the job market after graduation. If society does not lack, for example, journalists or psychologists, it is a bad idea to educate too many because they will then become unemployed.

Emne: [A-opf. Uddannelser, der kræver høje karakterer]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved, at der på mange uddannelser er en begrænsning på, hvor mange studerende der bliver optaget? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, det kan skabe store problemer. 

For det første er det unfair for de unge mennesker. Mange har en stor drøm om at blive for eksempel læge eller psykolog, men de kan ikke komme ind, fordi deres karakterer fra gymnasiet ikke er høje nok. Det kan gøre dem meget deprimerede eller stressede. 

For det andet kan samfundet miste mange dygtige folk. En person kan godt blive en fantastisk læge, selvom han eller hun ikke fik topkarakterer i fransk eller historie i gymnasiet.
Subject: [A-follow-up. Educations that require high grades] Do you think there can be any problems with the fact that on many study programs there is a limit on how many students are admitted? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think it can create big problems. 

Firstly, it is unfair to young people. Many have a big dream of becoming, for example, a doctor or a psychologist, but they cannot get in because their grades from high school are not high enough. This can make them very depressed or stressed. 

Secondly, society can lose many talented people. A person can easily become a fantastic doctor, even if he or she did not get top grades in French or history in high school.

Emne: [B-obl. Uddannelser med ledige pladser]Der er ofte ledige studiepladser på uddannelserne til lærer og pædagog, selvom der næsten er jobgaranti. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første har lærere og pædagoger et stort ansvar og meget travlt i hverdagen. Arbejdet kan være psykisk hårdt og stressende, fordi man arbejder med mange børn hver dag. 

For det andet synes mange unge, at lønnen er for lav i forhold til, hvor hårdt arbejdet er. De vil hellere vælge en uddannelse, hvor de kan tjene flere penge og have mere fleksibilitet.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Study programs with vacant spots] There are often vacant study spots on the teacher and pedagogue programs, even though there is almost a job guarantee. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, teachers and pedagogues have a great responsibility and are very busy in their daily lives. The work can be mentally tough and stressful because you work with many children every day. 

Secondly, many young people think that the salary is too low compared to how hard the work is. They would rather choose an education where they can earn more money and have more flexibility.

Emne: [B-opf. Uddannelser med ledige pladser]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved, at der er ledige studiepladser på uddannelserne til lærer og pædagog. Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, det kan blive et kæmpe problem for hele samfundet. 

For det første kommer vi til at mangle uddannede folk på skoler og i børnehaver i fremtiden. Det betyder, at der bliver færre voksne til at hjælpe børnene, og så falder kvaliteten. 

For det andet kan de lærere og pædagoger, som allerede arbejder der, blive meget stressede, fordi de skal løbe hurtigere og lave ekstra arbejde. Det kan give mere sygefravær og skabe en dårlig stemning.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Study programs with vacant spots] Do you think there can be any problems with the fact that there are vacant study spots on the teacher and pedagogue programs? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think it can become a huge problem for the whole of society. 

Firstly, we will lack trained people in schools and kindergartens in the future. This means that there will be fewer adults to help the children, and then the quality drops. 

Secondly, the teachers and pedagogues who already work there can become very stressed because they have to run faster and do extra work. This can lead to more sickness absence and create a bad atmosphere.

Emne: [A-obl. Brugerbetaling på museer]Nogle danskere mener, at det skal være gratis at gå på museum. Hvorfor tror du, de mener det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om kultur og uddannelse. Museer viser vores historie og kunst, og det er vigtigt, at alle mennesker har mulighed for at lære om det. Hvis det er gratis, kan familier med færre penge også tage deres børn med på museum. 

For det andet tror jeg, folk mener, at vi allerede betaler for museerne over skatten. Derfor synes de ikke, det er fair, at man skal betale for en billet en gang til ved indgangen.
Subject: [A-compulsory. User fees at museums] Some Danes believe that it should be free to go to museums. Why do you think they think that?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about culture and education. Museums show our history and art, and it is important that all people have the opportunity to learn about it. If it is free, families with less money can also take their children to museums. 

Secondly, I think people believe that we already pay for the museums through our taxes. Therefore, they do not think it is fair that you have to pay for a ticket a second time at the entrance.

Emne: [A-opf. Brugerbetaling på museer]Hvad tror du, det betyder for folks interesse for kunst og kultur, at det ikke er gratis at gå på museum?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, når det koster penge at gå på museum, vælger mange familier og studerende det fra, fordi de skal spare. Det betyder, at kunst og kultur bliver noget, som mest er for folk med mange penge. 

Hvis det var gratis, tror jeg, at mange flere mennesker ville blive nysgerrige og besøge museerne i deres fritid, fordi det var nemt og uforpligtende.
Subject: [A-follow-up. User fees at museums] What do you think it means for people's interest in art and culture that it is not free to go to museums?
Presentation:
I think when it costs money to go to a museum, many families and students choose not to go because they need to save money. This means that art and culture become something that is mostly for people with a lot of money. 

If it were free, I think that many more people would become curious and visit the museums in their free time because it would be easy and without commitment.

Emne: [B-obl. Gratis adgang til biblioteker]I Danmark er det gratis at bruge bibliotekerne. Hvorfor tror du, det er det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om uddannelse og viden til alle. I Danmark synes man, at alle mennesker skal have de samme muligheder for at læse bøger, lære noget nyt og studere, uanset om de har mange eller få penge. 

For det andet fungerer biblioteket som et godt mødested i byen. Det er et sted, hvor man kan læse avis, låne en computer eller tage sine børn med hen for at hygge sig. Det styrker fællesskabet i samfundet.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Free access to libraries] In Denmark, it is free to use the libraries. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about education and knowledge for everyone. In Denmark, people believe that everyone should have the same opportunities to read books, learn something new, and study, regardless of whether they have a lot or little money. 

Secondly, the library functions as a good meeting place in the city. It is a place where you can read the newspaper, borrow a computer, or take your children to have a cozy time. It strengthens the community in society.

Emne: [B-opf. Gratis adgang til biblioteker]Hvad tror du, det betyder for folks interesse for at læse, at det er gratis at låne bøger på bibliotekerne?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det betyder rigtig meget for folks interesse, især for børn og unge. 

Når det er gratis, er det nemt at gå på biblioteket og finde en spændende bog. Man behøver ikke at tænke på penge, og hvis man låner en bog, som er kedelig, kan man bare aflevere den igen uden at have tabt penge. 

Det gør, at folk læser mere i deres fritid. Hvis man skulle betale for det hele selv, tror jeg, at mange mennesker ville stoppe med at læse, fordi bøger kan være ret dyre i dag.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Free access to libraries] What do you think it means for people's interest in reading that it is free to borrow books at the libraries?
Presentation:
I think it means a very great deal for people's interest, especially for children and young people. 

When it is free, it is easy to go to the library and find an exciting book. You do not have to think about money, and if you borrow a book that is boring, you can just return it without having lost any money. 

This makes people read more in their free time. If you had to pay for everything yourself, I think that many people would stop reading because books can be quite expensive today.

Emne: [A-obl.]Nogle studerende bruger tid på at lave frivilligt ulønnet arbejde, selvom de også har travlt med deres studier. Hvorfor tror du, de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at mange studerende arbejder frivilligt, fordi de gerne vil få erfaring til deres CV. Når man søger job bagefter, er det godt at vise, at man har lavet noget praktisk. 

Desuden er det en rigtig god måde at møde nye mennesker på og få et netværk. Selvom de har travlt med at læse, giver det frivillige arbejde dem en pause fra bøgerne, og de føler, at de gør en positiv forskel for andre.
Subject: [A-compulsory] Some students spend time doing voluntary unpaid work, even though they are also busy with their studies. Why do you think they do that?
Presentation:
I think that many students do voluntary work because they want to get experience for their CV. When you look for a job afterwards, it is good to show that you have done something practical. 

Furthermore, it is a really good way to meet new people and build a network. Even though they are busy studying, the voluntary work gives them a break from the books, and they feel that they are making a positive difference for others.

Emne: [A-opf.]Hvad tror du, det betyder for studerendes fremtidige jobmuligheder, at de laver frivilligt arbejde?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det betyder rigtig meget. Når studerende laver frivilligt arbejde, viser de en fremtidig arbejdsgiver, at de er engagerede og kan tage ansvar. De lærer også at samarbejde med forskellige mennesker, og det er en vigtig kompetence på en arbejdsplads. 

Desuden kan det frivillige arbejde give dem specifikke færdigheder, som de ikke lærer på studiet, hvilket gør deres CV mere attraktivt.
Subject: [A-follow-up] What do you think it means for students' future job opportunities that they do voluntary work?
Presentation:
I think it means a great deal. When students do voluntary work, they show a future employer that they are committed and can take responsibility. They also learn to cooperate with different people, and that is an important skill in a workplace. 

Furthermore, the voluntary work can give them specific skills that they do not learn in their studies, which makes their CV more attractive.

Emne: [B-obl.]Nogle danskere laver frivilligt ulønnet arbejde, når de er gået på pension. Hvorfor tror du, de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at mange pensionister arbejder frivilligt, fordi de gerne vil have noget at lave i hverdagen. Når man stopper med at arbejde, kan dagen godt blive lang. Det frivillige arbejde giver dem en god grund til at komme ud af huset. 

Derudover er det en god måde at være social på og møde andre mennesker, så de ikke føler sig ensomme. Til sidst giver det dem en følelse af, at de stadig er nyttige og gør en forskel for samfundet.
Subject: [B-compulsory] Some Danes do voluntary unpaid work when they have retired. Why do you think they do that?
Presentation:
I think that many pensioners do voluntary work because they want to have something to do in their daily life. When you stop working, the day can feel quite long. The voluntary work gives them a good reason to get out of the house. 

In addition, it is a good way to be social and meet other people, so they do not feel lonely. Finally, it gives them a feeling that they are still useful and are making a difference for society.

Emne: [B-opf.]Hvad tror du, det betyder for pensionisters livskvalitet, at de laver frivilligt ulønnet arbejde?
Praesentation: Jeg tror, det betyder rigtig meget for deres livskvalitet. Når man bliver pensionist, mister man den daglige struktur, som et arbejde giver. Det frivillige arbejde giver dem noget fast at stå op til og et formål med dagen. De føler sig aktive og sunde, fordi de bruger både hovedet og kroppen. Det giver dem også meget livsglæde at hjælpe andre og føle, at samfundet stadig har brug for dem.
Subject: [B-follow-up] What do you think it means for pensioners' quality of life that they do voluntary unpaid work?
Presentation: I think it means a great deal for their quality of life. When you become a pensioner, you lose the daily structure that a job provides. The voluntary work gives them something permanent to get up for and a purpose for the day. They feel active and healthy because they use both their mind and their body. It also gives them a lot of joy in life to help others and feel that society still needs them.

Emne: [Obl.]På mange institutioner, fx plejehjem, skoler og museer, bruger man frivillige udover den professionelle arbejdskraft. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at de frivillige har ekstra tid til de mennesker, der er der. For eksempel på et plejehjem kan en frivillig gå en tur eller snakke med de ældre, hvilket de ansatte ofte ikke har tid til. Det giver mere livsglæde. 

Men en ulempe kan være, at de frivillige ikke har den rigtige uddannelse. Hvis der sker noget svært, ved de måske ikke, hvad de skal gøre. 

Desuden skal man passe på, at de frivillige ikke tager det arbejde, som rigtige medarbejdere skal have løn for.
Subject: [Compulsory] At many institutions, e.g., nursing homes, schools, and museums, volunteers are used in addition to the professional workforce. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that the volunteers have extra time for the people who are there. For example, at a nursing home, a volunteer can go for a walk or talk with the elderly, which the employees often do not have time for. It gives more joy in life. 

However, a disadvantage can be that the volunteers do not have the right education. If something difficult happens, they might not know what to do. 

Furthermore, one must be careful that volunteers do not take the work that real employees should be paid for.

Emne: [Obf.]Hvilke typer af frivilligt arbejde mener du er mest nyttige for et velfærdssamfund som det danske? Hvorfor?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at frivilligt arbejde på plejehjem og i sundhedssektoren er meget nyttigt. Danmark har mange ældre mennesker, og de ansatte har ofte for travlt. Når frivillige besøger de ældre, bekæmper det ensomhed. 

En anden vigtig type er lektiehjælp til børn og unge, især dem med anden etnisk baggrund. Det hjælper dem med at klare sig godt i skolen, hvilket er vigtigt for samfundets fremtid. Begge dele støtter velfærdssystemet, hvor der mangler hænder.
Subject: [Follow-up] Which types of voluntary work do you think are most useful for a welfare society like the Danish one? Why?
Presentation:
I think that voluntary work in nursing homes and the healthcare sector is very useful. Denmark has many elderly people, and the staff are often too busy. When volunteers visit the elderly, it fights loneliness. 

Another important type is homework help for children and young people, especially those from a different ethnic background. It helps them do well in school, which is important for the future of society. Both parts support the welfare system, where there is a shortage of hands.

Emne: [Obj.] Daginstitutioner er offentligt støttede i Danmark. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det for det danske samfund?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er både fordele og ulemper ved det. 

Den største fordel er, at det er billigt for forældrene at få deres børn passet. Det betyder, at både mor og far kan gå på arbejde og tjene penge, hvilket er godt for samfundets økonomi. 

Desuden sikrer det, at alle børn får de samme muligheder, uanset hvor mange penge deres forældre har. 

Den største ulempe er dog, at det koster staten rigtig mange penge i skat. Når mange børn går i institution, kan der også mangle personale, og det kan betyde, at der er mindre tid til det enkelte barn.
Subject: [Obj.] Daycare institutions are publicly subsidized in Denmark. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there could be for Danish society?
Presentation:
I think there are both advantages and disadvantages to this. 

The main advantage is that it is cheap for parents to have their children cared for. This means that both mom and dad can go to work and earn money, which is good for the society's economy. 

Furthermore, it ensures that all children get the same opportunities, regardless of how much money their parents have. 

However, the main disadvantage is that it costs the state a lot of money in taxes. When many children go to daycare, there can also be a shortage of staff, and that can mean less time for the individual child.

Emne: [Opf.] Daginstitutioner er offentligt støttede, men ikke gratis. Mener du, at de skal være det? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg synes, at daginstitutioner skal være helt gratis i Danmark. 

For det første vil det hjælpe mange børnefamilier, som har færre penge, fordi det er dyrt at have børn i institution i dag. Det vil give alle børn de samme muligheder fra starten. 

For det andet vil det gøre det nemmere for forældre at arbejde fuldtid, hvilket er godt for samfundet. 

På den anden side ved jeg godt, at det vil koste staten mange penge, og skatten vil måske stige. Men jeg mener stadig, det er en god investering i børnenes fremtid.
Subject: [Follow-up] Daycare institutions are publicly subsidized, but not free. Do you think they should be? Why/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think that daycare institutions should be completely free in Denmark. 

Firstly, it would help many families with children who have less money, because it is expensive to have children in daycare today. It would give all children the same opportunities from the start. 

Secondly, it would make it easier for parents to work full-time, which is good for society. 

On the other hand, I know well that it will cost the state a lot of money, and taxes might increase. But I still believe it is a good investment in the children's future.

Emne: [A-obl.]Nogle mennesker går sommetider på arbejde, selvom de er forkølede. Hvorfor tror du, de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første har mange medarbejdere rigtig travlt på deres job. De har mange opgaver, og de vil ikke have, at deres kolleger skal lave ekstra arbejde, fordi de er syge. De føler et stort ansvar. 

For det andet tænker mange, at en lille forkølelse ikke er en rigtig sygdom. De føler sig raske nok til at sidde foran computeren, især hvis de kan arbejde hjemmefra, så de ikke smitter andre på kontoret.
Subject: [A-compulsory] Some people sometimes go to work even though they have a cold. Why do you think they do that?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, many employees are very busy at their jobs. They have many tasks, and they do not want their colleagues to do extra work because they are sick. They feel a great responsibility. 

Secondly, many people think that a minor cold is not a real illness. They feel healthy enough to sit in front of the computer, especially if they can work from home so they do not infect others in the office.

Emne: [A-opf.]Hvad tror du, det betyder for ens forhold til kollegerne, at man nogle gange går på arbejde, selvom man er forkølet?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan betyde både gode og dårlige ting. 

På den ene side kan kollegerne blive glade, fordi man hjælper med opgaverne, så de ikke skal løbe stærkere. Det viser, at man er en god holdspiller. 

På den anden side kan kollegerne også blive irriterede eller vrede, fordi man risikerer at smitte dem. Hvis de bliver syge, kan det gå ud over deres egne familier. 

Derfor er det bedst at aftale det med sine kolleger eller arbejde hjemmefra.
Subject: [A-follow-up] What do you think it means for one's relationship with colleagues that one sometimes goes to work even though one has a cold?
Presentation:
I think it can mean both good and bad things. 

On one hand, colleagues might be happy because you help with the tasks, so they do not have to run faster. It shows that you are a good team player. 

On the other hand, colleagues can also get irritated or angry because you risk infecting them. If they get sick, it can affect their own families. 

Therefore, it is best to agree on it with your colleagues or work from home.

Emne: [B-obl.]Pædagoger og sygehjælpere har et højere sygefravær end ingeniører og konsulenter. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første har pædagoger og sygehjælpere et meget fysisk arbejde. De løfter tunge ting, går meget og arbejder tæt sammen med syge mennesker og børn. Derfor bliver de nemmere smittet med sygdomme, eller de får ondt i kroppen. 

For det andet har ingeniører og konsulenter mere fleksibilitet. Hvis de føler sig lidt syge, kan de bare arbejde hjemmefra foran computeren. Det kan en pædagog eller en sygehjælper ikke.
Subject: [B-compulsory] Pedagogical staff and nurse's aides have a higher sickness absence than engineers and consultants. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, pedagogical staff and nurse's aides have a very physical job. They lift heavy things, walk a lot, and work closely with sick people and children. Therefore, they get infected with illnesses more easily, or they get body aches. 

Secondly, engineers and consultants have more flexibility. If they feel a little sick, they can just work from home in front of the computer. A pedagogue or a nurse's aide cannot do that.

Emne: [B-opf.]Hvad tror du, det betyder for ens forhold til kollegerne, at man har et højt sygefravær?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan være svært for forholdet til kollegerne. Når en person tit er syg, skal de andre kolleger løbe hurtigere og lave ekstra arbejde. Hvis det sker ofte, kan de andre medarbejdere blive stressede, trætte eller irriterede. Det kan skabe en dårlig stemning på arbejdspladsen. 

Men hvis man er åben og taler med sine kolleger om problemet, vil de fleste dog have forståelse for, at man er syg.
Subject: [B-follow-up] What do you think it means for one's relationship with colleagues that one has a high sickness absence?
Presentation:
I think it can be difficult for the relationship with colleagues. When a person is often sick, the other colleagues have to run faster and do extra work. If this happens frequently, the other employees can become stressed, tired, or irritated. This can create a bad atmosphere in the workplace. 

However, if you are open and talk to your colleagues about the problem, most people will understand that you are sick.

Emne: [Obl.]Nogle virksomheder gør meget for at reducere de ansattes sygefravær. Virksomhederne tilbyder for eksempel gratis lægetjek og rådgivning om kost og motion. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved, at de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at de ansatte bliver mere sunde og glade. Når virksomheden hjælper med kost og motion, får medarbejderne mere energi, og så bliver de sjældnere syge. Det er godt for både chefen og arbejderne. 

Men en ulempe kan være, at medarbejderne føler et stort pres. De kan føle, at chefen blander sig for meget i deres privatliv og bestemmer, hvad de skal spise. Det er vigtigt, at det er frivilligt for de ansatte.
Subject: [Compulsory] Some companies do a lot to reduce employees' sickness absence. For example, companies offer free medical check-ups and advice on diet and exercise. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to them doing this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that the employees become healthier and happier. When the company helps with diet and exercise, the employees get more energy, and then they get sick less often. It is good for both the boss and the workers. 

However, a disadvantage can be that the employees feel a great deal of pressure. They might feel that the boss is interfering too much in their private life and deciding what they should eat. It is important that it is voluntary for the employees.

Emne: [Obf.]Synes du, at det er i orden, at en virksomhed blander sig i de ansattes livsstil, eller bør ens livsstil være en privat sag? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Jeg mener, at ens livsstil primært bør være en privat sag. Hvad man spiser, og hvad man gør i sin fritid, er ens eget valg. Hvis chefen blander sig for meget, kan man føle sig overvåget og stresset på sit arbejde. 

Men det er i orden, hvis virksomheden kommer med gode, frivillige tilbud, som for eksempel sund mad i kantinen eller fitnessrabat. Så længe det ikke er tvang, kan det være en hjælp for de ansatte.
Subject: [Follow-up] Do you think it is okay for a company to interfere in the employees' lifestyle, or should one's lifestyle be a private matter? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
I believe that one's lifestyle should primarily be a private matter. What you eat and what you do in your free time is your own choice. If the boss interferes too much, you can feel monitored and stressed at your job. 

However, it is okay if the company comes up with good, voluntary offers, such as healthy food in the canteen or fitness discounts. As long as it is not mandatory, it can be a help to the employees.

Emne: [A-obl.]Færre og færre danskere køber og læser avis. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første har teknologien ændret sig meget. I dag kan folk læse alle nyhederne gratis og hurtigt på deres telefoner eller computere, så de behøver ikke at købe en fysisk avis. 

For det andet har folk mindre tid i hverdagen. Det tager lang tid at læse en hel avis, og mange foretrækker bare at læse korte overskrifter på de sociale medier eller se nyheder i fjernsynet. Det er bare nemmere og billigere.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Fewer and fewer Danes buy and read newspapers] Fewer and fewer Danes buy and read newspapers. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, technology has changed a lot. Today, people can read all the news for free and quickly on their phones or computers, so they do not need to buy a physical newspaper. 

Secondly, people have less time in their daily lives. It takes a long time to read a whole newspaper, and many prefer to just read short headlines on social media or watch the news on television. It is simply easier and cheaper.

Emne: [A-opf.]Kan der efter din mening være nogen problemer ved, at færre og færre danskere køber og læser avis? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, der kan være et problem med falske nyheder. Når folk ikke læser rigtige aviser, finder de ofte deres nyheder på sociale medier som Facebook eller TikTok. Her kan alle skrive hvad som helst, og historierne er ikke altid sande. 

En anden ulempe er, at man kun læser korte overskrifter i stedet for at forstå en hel sag. Det kan gøre det svært for folk at diskutere politik og samfund på en god måde.
Subject: [A-follow-up] In your opinion, can there be any problems with fewer and fewer Danes buying and reading newspapers? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be a problem with fake news. When people do not read real newspapers, they often find their news on social media like Facebook or TikTok. Here, anyone can write anything, and the stories are not always true. 

Another disadvantage is that people only read short headlines instead of understanding a whole case. This can make it difficult for people to discuss politics and society in a good way.

Emne: [B-obl.]Flere og flere danskere får deres nyheder gennem online-medier. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det skyldes, at online-medier er utrolig hurtige og nemme at bruge. I dag har næsten alle en smartphone i lommen, og man kan læse nyheder hvor som helst og når som helst – for eksempel i bussen eller i en pause på arbejdet. 

Desuden opdateres de digitale nyheder hele tiden, så man altid ved, hvad der sker lige nu. Mange online-nyheder er også gratis, og det passer folk rigtig godt, fordi de gerne vil spare penge.
Subject: [B-compulsory] More and more Danes get their news through online media. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think it is because online media is incredibly fast and easy to use. Today, almost everyone has a smartphone in their pocket, and you can read the news anywhere and at any time – for example, on the bus or during a break at work. 

Furthermore, digital news is updated constantly, so you always know what is happening right now. Many online news stories are also free, and that suits people very well because they want to save money.

Emne: [B-opf.]Kan der efter din mening være nogen problemer ved, at flere og flere danskere får deres nyheder gennem online-medier? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, der kan være nogle problemer. 

Den største ulempe er, at der er mange falske nyheder på internettet. På sociale medier kan alle dele historier, som ikke er sande, og det kan være svært at se forskel på sandhed og løgn. 

Et andet problem er, at artikler på nettet ofte er meget korte. Folk læser tit kun overskriften, og så forstår de ikke hele sagen. Det kan gøre det svært at have en god debat om samfundet.
Subject: [B-follow-up] In your opinion, can there be any problems with more and more Danes getting their news through online media? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be some problems. 

The biggest disadvantage is that there is a lot of fake news on the internet. On social media, anyone can share stories that are not true, and it can be difficult to tell the difference between truth and lies. 

Another problem is that articles on the web are often very short. People frequently only read the headline, and then they do not understand the whole case. This can make it difficult to have a good debate about society.

Emne: [Obl.]Med blogs og andre online-medier er det i dag nemmere for almindelige mennesker at deltage i den offentlige debat. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at alle kan sige deres mening nu. Før i tiden var det kun politikere og journalister, der talte i medierne, men i dag kan almindelige mennesker også dele deres idéer. Det gør demokratiet stærkere. 

Men en stor ulempe er, at tonen på internettet ofte er meget hård og grim. Mange skriver grimme kommentarer, fordi de sidder bag en skærm. Desuden kan folk nemt sprede rygter og falske nyheder, som ikke passer.
Subject: [Compulsory] With blogs and other online media, it is easier today for ordinary people to participate in the public debate. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this?
Presentation:
