I think that the biggest advantage is that everyone can express their opinion now. In the past, it was only politicians and journalists who spoke in the media, but today ordinary people can also share their ideas. This makes democracy stronger. 

However, a major disadvantage is that the tone on the internet is often very harsh and nasty. Many people write mean comments because they are sitting behind a screen. Furthermore, people can easily spread rumors and fake news that are not true.

Emne: [Obf.]Hvad tror du, det betyder for demokratiet, at det i dag er nemmere for almindelige mennesker at deltage i den offentlige debat? Hvorfor?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det betyder meget for demokratiet, fordi det bliver mere åbent. Når almindelige mennesker kan skrive på nettet, kan politikerne bedre høre, hvad befolkningen tænker og har brug for. Det gør, at flere føler sig som en del af samfundet. 

Men det kan også være et problem, hvis debatten bliver for hård, eller hvis folk deler falske informationer. 

Så jeg tror, det er godt for demokratiet, hvis vi lærer at tale pænt til hinanden online.
Subject: [Follow-up] What do you think it means for democracy that it is easier today for ordinary people to participate in the public debate? Why?
Presentation:
I think it means a lot for democracy because it becomes more open. When ordinary people can write on the internet, politicians can better hear what the population thinks and needs. This makes more people feel like a part of society. 

However, it can also be a problem if the debate becomes too harsh, or if people share false information. 

Therefore, I think it is good for democracy if we learn to speak politely to each other online.

Emne: [B-obl. Klima og ferievaner]Nogle rejser meget med fly, selvom de godt ved, at det er skadeligt for klimaet. Hvorfor tror du, de alligevel gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er flere grunde til, at folk stadig flyver. Først og fremmest er det ofte den hurtigste og nemmeste måde at rejse på, hvis man vil besøge lande, der ligger langt væk. Hvis man for eksempel kun har to ugers ferie, vil man ikke bruge flere dage i et tog eller en bil. 

Derudover er flybilletter tit meget billige, nogle gange billigere end togbilletter. Mange mennesker prioriterer deres egne oplevelser og behov for at slappe af i solen højere end klimaet lige i det øjeblik.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Climate and holiday habits] Some people travel a lot by plane, even though they well know that it is harmful to the climate. Why do you think they do it anyway?
Presentation:
I think there are several reasons why people still fly. First and foremost, it is often the fastest and easiest way to travel if you want to visit countries that are far away. If, for example, you only have two weeks of vacation, you do not want to spend several days in a train or a car. 

In addition, plane tickets are often very cheap, sometimes cheaper than train tickets. Many people prioritize their own experiences and need to relax in the sun higher than the climate just at that moment.

Emne: [B-opf. Klima og ferievaner]Tror du, folk også vil rejse meget med fly i fremtiden? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, at folk stadig vil rejse meget med fly i fremtiden. Verden er blevet meget global, og mange mennesker har familie eller arbejde i andre lande. 

Men jeg tror også, at måden, vi flyver på, vil ændre sig. For eksempel vil flyene nok blive mere miljøvenlige, og måske bliver det dyrere at flyve, så folk tænker sig mere om. Men flyet er bare så hurtigt og nemt, at vi ikke kan undvære det.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Climate and holiday habits] Do you think people will also travel a lot by plane in the future? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think that people will still travel a lot by plane in the future. The world has become very global, and many people have family or work in other countries. 

However, I also think that the way we fly will change. For example, planes will probably become more environmentally friendly, and perhaps it will become more expensive to fly, so people will think more carefully. But the plane is just so fast and easy that we cannot do without it.

Emne: [Obg.]Det er især unge, der har fokus på at leve klimavenligt. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at de unge er gode til at finde på nye, grønne idéer. De kan ændre fremtiden og lære deres forældre at passe bedre på jorden, for eksempel ved at spise mindre kød. 

Men en ulempe kan være, at det kan give de unge klimaangst eller stress. De føler måske, at det hele er deres ansvar. 

Desuden kan det være svært og dyrt for en ung studerende kun at købe økologisk og klimavenlig mad.
Subject: [Compulsory] It is especially young people who focus on living in a climate-friendly way. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that young people are good at coming up with new, green ideas. They can change the future and teach their parents to take better care of the earth, for example by eating less meat. 

However, a disadvantage can be that it can cause climate anxiety or stress for young people. They might feel that it is all their responsibility. 

Furthermore, it can be difficult and expensive for a young student to only buy organic and climate-friendly food.

Emne: [Opf.]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved at prioritere klima frem for velfærd? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, der kan opstå store problemer, hvis man kun tænker på klimaet. Hvis staten bruger alle pengene på grøn energi og miljøprojekter, vil der være færre penge til vigtige ting som hospitaler, plejehjem og skoler. Det kan betyde, at de ældre og syge ikke får den hjælp, de har brug for i hverdagen. 

Derfor er det vigtigt at finde en god balance, så vi både passer på planeten og på de mennesker, der bor her.
Subject: [Follow-up] Do you think there can be any problems with prioritizing climate over welfare? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think big problems can arise if we only think about the climate. If the state spends all the money on green energy and environmental projects, there will be less money for important things like hospitals, nursing homes, and schools. This could mean that the elderly and sick do not get the help they need in their daily lives. 

Therefore, it is important to find a good balance so that we take care of both the planet and the people who live here.

Emne: [Obg.]Der er nogle unge, der tager et eller to års pause efter gymnasiet, før de vælger uddannelse. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det for samfundet?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at de unge bliver mere modne. Når de tager et sabbatår for at arbejde eller rejse, lærer de meget om sig selv. Det betyder, at de bagefter vælger den rigtige uddannelse, og så dropper de sjældnere ud af studiet. Det sparer samfundet for mange penge. 

Men en stor ulempe er, at de unge kommer senere ud på arbejdsmarkedet. Hvis alle venter to år med at studere, mangler samfundet arbejdskraft, og staten får færre penge ind i skat, fordi folk arbejder i færre år i alt.
Subject: [Compulsory. Break after high school] There are some young people who take one or two years of a break after high school before they choose an education. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this for society?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that the young people become more mature. When they take a gap year to work or travel, they learn a lot about themselves. This means that afterwards, they choose the right education, and then they drop out of their studies less often. This saves society a lot of money. 

However, a major disadvantage is that young people enter the job market later. If everyone waits two years to study, society will lack labor, and the state will receive less money in taxes because people work for fewer years in total.

Emne: [Opf.]Hvad tror du, det betyder for de unges uddannelsesvalg, at de har holdt et eller to års pause?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det betyder, at de unge træffer et bedre og mere modent valg. Når de har haft en pause, har de haft tid til at tænke sig om og finde ud af, hvad de rigtig gerne vil. De har måske haft et arbejde og prøvet virkeligheden af, og det gør dem mere klar til at studere. 

Men på den anden side kan en ulempe være, at nogle unge helt glemmer, hvordan man går i skole. De kan miste vanen med at læse og lave lektier, og så kan det være svært at starte på en uddannelse igen.
Subject: [Follow-up. Break after high school] What do you think it means for young people's choice of education that they have taken a one or two-year break?
Presentation:
I think it means that young people make a better and more mature choice. When they have had a break, they have had time to think things over and find out what they really want to do. They might have had a job and tested out reality, and that makes them more ready to study. 

But on the other hand, a disadvantage can be that some young people completely forget how to go to school. They can lose the habit of reading and doing homework, and then it can be difficult to start an education again.

Emne: [A-obl. Flere el-cykler i trafikken]Der er flere og flere danskere, både unge og gamle, der køber en elcykel i stedet for en almindelig cykel. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er flere grunde til, at elcykler er blevet så populære i Danmark. 

For det første er det meget nemmere at cykle langt eller op ad bakke, fordi man får hjælp fra motoren. Det betyder, at ældre mennesker kan blive ved med at cykle, selvom de ikke har så mange kræfter. 

For det andet er det godt for de unge, fordi de kan cykle hurtigt til skole eller arbejde uden at blive trætte og svedige. 

Til sidst tænker mange danskere også på miljøet. De vælger elcyklen i stedet for bilen, fordi det er billigere og bedre for naturen, og man slipper for at sidde i bilkø.
Subject: [A-comp. More e-bikes in traffic]There are more and more Danes, both young and old, who buy an e-bike instead of a regular bicycle. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are several reasons why e-bikes have become so popular in Denmark. 

Firstly, it is much easier to cycle long distances or uphill because you get help from the motor. This means that older people can keep cycling, even if they don't have as much strength. 

Secondly, it is good for young people because they can cycle quickly to school or work without getting tired and sweaty. 

Finally, many Danes also think about the environment. They choose the e-bike instead of the car because it is cheaper and better for nature, and you avoid sitting in traffic jams.

Emne: [A-opf. Flere el-cykler i trafikken]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved, at flere og flere danskere køber en elcykel i stedet for en almindelig cykel – fx for trafiksikkerheden eller sundheden? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, der kan være nogle problemer. 

For det første kører en elcykel meget hurtigt, og det kan være farligt for trafiksikkerheden. Der sker nemt ulykker på cykelstien, fordi de andre cyklister ikke regner med den høje fart, især når det er ældre mennesker, der cykler. 

For det andet kan det være dårligt for sundheden. Man bevæger sig mindre og bruger færre kræfter, når cyklen har en motor. Det er sundere for kroppen at bruge en almindelig cykel, hvor man skal træde hårdt i pedalerne.
Subject: [A-follow-up. More e-bikes in traffic] Do you think there can be any problems with more and more Danes buying an e-bike instead of a regular bicycle – for example, for traffic safety or health? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be some problems. 

Firstly, an e-bike rides very fast, and that can be dangerous for traffic safety. Accidents easily happen on the bike path because the other cyclists do not expect the high speed, especially when it is elderly people cycling. 

Secondly, it can be bad for health. You move less and use less effort when the bike has a motor. It is healthier for the body to use a regular bicycle where you have to pedal hard.

Emne: [B-obl. Mere plads il cyklister og fodgængere]Der er nogle, der mener, at der skal være mere plads til fodgængere og cyklister i de større byer. Hvorfor tror du, de mener det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om miljøet og klimaet. Hvis der er mere plads til cykler og fodgængere, vil flere lade bilen stå derhjemme. Det betyder mindre CO2 og renere luft i byerne. 

For det andet handler det om sundhed og god plads. Det er sundt for folk at røre sig, og byerne bliver hyggeligere og mere sikre at bo i, når der ikke er så mange biler, der støjer og kører stærkt.
Subject: [B-compulsory. More space for cyclists and pedestrians] There are some who believe that there should be more space for pedestrians and cyclists in the larger cities. Why do you think they believe that?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about the environment and the climate. If there is more space for bicycles and pedestrians, more people will leave their car at home. This means less CO2 and cleaner air in the cities. 

Secondly, it is about health and plenty of space. It is healthy for people to move around, and the cities become cozier and safer to live in when there are not so many cars that make noise and drive fast.

Emne: [B-opf. Mere plads il cyklister og fodgængere]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved at give mere plads til fodgængere og cyklister i de større byer? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation: Ja, jeg tror, det kan skabe problemer for butikkerne og bilerne. Hvis man fjerner veje og parkeringspladser for at lave cykelstier, bliver det meget svært for biler at køre i byen. Det betyder, at folk, der bor langt væk, måske stopper med at tage ind til byen for at købe ind. Butikkerne kan miste mange kunder og tjene færre penge. Desuden kan det blive svært for lastbiler at levere varer til supermarkederne.
Subject: [B-follow-up. More space for cyclists and pedestrians] Do you think there can be any problems with giving more space to pedestrians and cyclists in the larger cities? Why?/Why not?
Presentation: Yes, I think it can create problems for shops and cars. If roads and parking spaces are removed to make bike paths, it will be very difficult for cars to drive in the city. This means that people who live far away might stop going into the city to shop. The shops could lose many customers and earn less money. Furthermore, it can become difficult for trucks to deliver goods to the supermarkets.

Emne: [Obl.]Der er nogle, der mener, at der skal investeres i hurtigtog i hele landet. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det – fx for miljøet?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at det er rigtig godt for miljøet. Hvis vi har hurtige tog, vil mange mennesker vælge at tage toget i stedet for at køre i bil eller tage et fly. Det betyder mindre CO2 og mindre forurening. 

En anden fordel er, at det bliver meget nemmere og hurtigere at rejse mellem de store byer. 

Men en stor ulempe er, at det er utrolig dyrt at bygge nye skinner til hurtigtog. Det koster mange milliarder kroner, og de penge kunne samfundet måske bruge på hospitaler eller skoler i stedet for.
Subject: [Compulsory. High-speed trains across the country] There are some who believe that there should be investments in high-speed trains across the country. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this – for example, for the environment?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that it is really good for the environment. If we have fast trains, many people will choose to take the train instead of driving a car or taking a flight. This means less CO2 and less pollution. 

Another advantage is that it becomes much easier and faster to travel between the big cities. 

However, a major disadvantage is that it is incredibly expensive to build new tracks for high-speed trains. It costs many billions of kroner, and society could perhaps use that money on hospitals or schools instead.

Emne: [Opf.]Tror du, det vil være bedre at investere i flere motorveje end i hurtigtog i hele landet? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Nej, det tror jeg ikke. Jeg mener, det er bedre at investere i hurtigtog. Den vigtigste grund er klimaet og miljøet. Biler på motorveje forurener rigtig meget, mens tog er en meget grønnere løsning. Hvis vi bygger flere motorveje, får vi bare flere biler og mere CO2. 

Men på den anden side kan motorveje være gode for folk, der bor på landet, hvor der ikke kører tog. 

Alligevel synes jeg, vi skal tænke på fremtiden og vælge toget.
Subject: [Follow-up. High-speed trains across the country] Do you think it would be better to invest in more highways than in high-speed trains across the country? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
No, I do not think so. I believe it is better to invest in high-speed trains. The most important reason is the climate and the environment. Cars on highways pollute a great deal, while trains are a much greener solution. If we build more highways, we will just get more cars and more CO2. 

However, on the other hand, highways can be good for people who live in the countryside where trains do not run. 

Still, I think we should think about the future and choose the train.

Emne: [A-obl. At arbejde på deltid]Der er nogle, der arbejder på deltid, selvom de er glade for deres job. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første vil mange mennesker gerne have mere tid til deres familie og børn. Hvis man arbejder mindre, har man mere energi til at være sammen med sine børn i hverdagen. 

For det andet vil nogle gerne have mere fritid til deres hobbyer eller til at slappe af. Selvom de er glade for deres arbejde, synes de, at livet også skal handle om andre ting end jobbet. Det giver dem en bedre balance i livet.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Working part-time] There are some people who work part-time, even though they are happy with their job. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that.

Firstly, many people would like to have more time for their family and children. If you work less, you have more energy to be together with your children in your daily life. 

Secondly, some people want to have more free time for their hobbies or to relax. Even though they are happy with their work, they think that life should also be about things other than the job. It gives them a better balance in life.

Emne: [A-opf. At arbejde på deltid]Hvad tror du, det kan betyde for ens karriere, at man arbejder på deltid?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan gøre det sværere at gøre karriere og få en højere stilling. Hvis man arbejder på deltid, er man ikke så meget på arbejdspladsen, og så går man glip af vigtige møder eller projekter. Chefen vil måske hellere give en forfremmelse til en kollega, der arbejder på fuldtid. 

Men på den anden side kan deltid betyde, at man ikke bliver stresset, og så kan man passe sit arbejde bedre i mange år.
Subject: [A-follow-up. Working part-time] What do you think it can mean for one's career that one works part-time?
Presentation:
I think it can make it harder to make a career and get a higher position. If you work part-time, you are not at the workplace as much, and then you miss out on important meetings or projects. The boss might prefer to give a promotion to a colleague who works full-time. 

But on the other hand, part-time can mean that you do not get stressed, and then you can do your job better for many years.

Emne: [B-obl. At arbejde meget]Der er nogle, der arbejder meget, selvom de har familie og børn. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to store grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om økonomi. Det er dyrt at have familie og børn, og mange vil gerne have penge nok til et godt hus, en bil og gode ferier. 

For det andet kan det være, fordi de er rigtig glade for deres arbejde. De har måske en spændende karriere, som betyder meget for dem, og det kan være svært at sige nej til ekstra opgaver. 

Det kan dog være svært at finde en god balance i hverdagen.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Working a lot] There are some people who work a lot, even though they have a family and children. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two big reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about finances. It is expensive to have a family and children, and many people want to have enough money for a good house, a car, and nice holidays. 

Secondly, it might be because they are really happy with their work. They might have an exciting career that means a lot to them, and it can be difficult to say no to extra tasks. 

However, it can be hard to find a good balance in daily life.

Emne: [B-opf. At arbejde meget]Hvad tror du, det kan betyde for forholdet til familie og børn, at man arbejder meget?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan betyde, at man får mindre tid sammen i hverdagen, og det kan skade forholdet. 

Når man arbejder meget, er man tit træt, når man kommer hjem. Så har man ikke så meget energi til at lege med sine børn eller tale med sin partner. Børnene kan føle sig ensomme eller tænke, at arbejdet er vigtigere end dem. 

Men hvis man tjener mange penge, kan man give sin familie gode oplevelser i ferierne, hvilket er positivt.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Working a lot] What do you think it can mean for the relationship with family and children that one works a lot?
Presentation:
I think it can mean that you get less time together in daily life, and that can damage the relationship. 

When you work a lot, you are often tired when you come home. Then you do not have much energy to play with your children or talk to your partner. The children might feel lonely or think that work is more important than them. 

However, if you earn a lot of money, you can give your family good experiences during the holidays, which is positive.

Emne: [Obg.]Der er nogle, der mener, at den gennemsnitlige ugentlige arbejdstid skal sættes ned til cirka 30 timer om ugen. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det for samfundet?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at folk vil få mindre stress og mere overskud i hverdagen. Når man arbejder mindre, har man mere tid til sin familie og sine børn, og det kan give raske og glade borgere. 

Men en stor ulempe kan være, at samfundet mister penge. Hvis folk arbejder færre timer, producerer virksomhederne mindre, og staten får mindre ind i skat. 

Desuden mangler vi allerede arbejdskraft i dag, for eksempel på plejehjem og hospitaler.
Subject: [Compulsory] There are some who believe that the average weekly working hours should be reduced to about 30 hours a week. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this for society?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that people will have less stress and more energy in their daily lives. When you work less, you have more time for your family and your children, and that can result in healthy and happy citizens. 

However, a major disadvantage can be that society loses money. If people work fewer hours, companies produce less, and the state receives less in taxes. 

Furthermore, we already have a shortage of labor today, for example in nursing homes and hospitals.

Emne: [Opf.]Hvad tror du, det vil betyde for medarbejdernes effektivitet, hvis den gennemsnitlige ugentlige arbejdstid sættes ned til cirka 30 timer om ugen?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at medarbejdernes effektivitet vil stige. Når man arbejder færre timer, er man mindre træt og har mere energi. Så kan man koncentrere sig bedre og nå det samme arbejde på kortere tid. Hvis man har en lang arbejdsuge, spilder man ofte tid, fordi man mister fokus. 

Men på den anden side kan det være svært at nå alle opgaver i nogle job, for eksempel hvis man arbejder i en butik eller på et sygehus, hvor man skal være fysisk til stede.
Subject: [Follow-up] What do you think it will mean for employees' efficiency if the average weekly working hours are reduced to about 30 hours a week?
Presentation:
I think that employees' efficiency will increase. When you work fewer hours, you are less tired and have more energy. Then you can concentrate better and achieve the same work in less time. If you have a long work week, you often waste time because you lose focus. 

But on the other hand, it can be difficult to manage all tasks in some jobs, for example if you work in a shop or at a hospital, where you have to be physically present.

Emne: [A-obl. Gratis at gå til læge]Der er nogle, der mener, at det ikke skal være helt gratis at gå til læge. De mener, at det er en god idé at indføre et gebyr ved lægebesøg. Hvorfor tror du, de mener det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første er der mange mennesker, som går til lægen med meget små problemer, som for eksempel en lille forkølelse. Hvis man skal betale et lille gebyr, vil folk måske tænke sig om en ekstra gang, før de bestiller en tid. 

For det andet kan pengene fra gebyret hjælpe sygehusene og lægerne. Samfundet mangler penge til sundhedsvæsenet, og de ekstra penge kan bruges til at give en bedre behandling til de patienter, som er meget syge.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Free to go to the doctor] There are some who believe that it should not be completely free to go to the doctor. They think that it is a good idea to introduce a fee for doctor visits. Why do you think they believe that?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, there are many people who go to the doctor with very small problems, such as a minor cold. If you have to pay a small fee, people might think twice before booking an appointment. 

Secondly, the money from the fee can help hospitals and doctors. Society lacks money for the healthcare system, and the extra money can be used to provide better treatment for those patients who are very sick.

Emne: [A-opf. Gratis at gå til læge]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved at indføre et gebyr ved lægebesøg? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation: Ja, jeg tror, det kan skabe store problemer, især for folk med færre penge. Hvis man skal betale for at gå til læge, vil fattige familier, pensionister eller studerende måske lade være med at bestille en tid, selvom de er syge. Det betyder, at deres sygdom kan blive meget værre, før de får hjælp. Det er ikke fair, for i et samfund som Danmark skal alle have den samme ret til at blive raske, uanset hvor mange penge de har på deres bankkonto.
Subject: Do you think there can be any problems with introducing a fee for doctor visits? Why?/Why not?
Presentation: Yes, I think it can create big problems, especially for people with less money. If you have to pay to go to the doctor, poor families, pensioners, or students might avoid booking an appointment, even if they are sick. This means that their illness can get much worse before they get help. That is not fair, because in a society like Denmark, everyone should have the same right to get well, regardless of how much money they have in their bank account.

Emne: [B-obl. Behandling på privathospital]Der er nogle, der vælger at betale for behandling på et privathospital. Hvorfor tror du, de vælger det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om tid. På de offentlige hospitaler er der tit lange ventelister, og man skal vente mange måneder på at blive opereret. På et privathospital kan man få hjælp med det samme. 

For det andet handler det om tryghed. Mange føler, at de får bedre tid med lægen og mere luksus på et privathospital. Selvom det koster mange penge, vælger folk det, fordi deres sundhed er det vigtigste for dem.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Treatment at private hospitals] There are some people who choose to pay for treatment at a private hospital. Why do you think they choose that?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about time. At public hospitals, there are often long waiting lists, and you have to wait many months to get surgery. At a private hospital, you can get help right away. 

Secondly, it is about comfort and security. Many feel that they get more time with the doctor and more luxury at a private hospital. Even though it costs a lot of money, people choose it because their health is the most important thing to them.

Emne: [B-opf. Behandling på privathospital]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved, at nogle ikke har råd til at betale for behandling på et privathospital? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, det kan skabe ulighed i samfundet. Når rige mennesker kan betale for hurtig hjælp på et privathospital, slipper de for de lange ventelister. 

Men folk med færre penge skal vente i mange måneder på det offentlige hospital. Det betyder, at de rige hurtigere bliver raske og kan komme tilbage på arbejde. 

Jeg synes, det er et problem, fordi alle mennesker skal have den samme ret til god og hurtig behandling, uanset hvor mange penge de har.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Treatment at private hospitals] Do you think there can be any problems with the fact that some people cannot afford to pay for treatment at a private hospital? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think it can create inequality in society. When rich people can pay for quick help at a private hospital, they avoid the long waiting lists. 

However, people with less money have to wait for many months at the public hospital. This means that the rich get well faster and can return to work. 

I think this is a problem because all people should have the same right to good and fast treatment, regardless of how much money they have.

Emne: [Obl.]Der bliver færre men større hospitaler i Danmark. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at de store hospitaler har bedre udstyr og mere eksperthjælp. Når lægerne er samlet ét sted, bliver de mere specialiserede, og så kan de give en bedre behandling til alvorligt syge mennesker. 

Men en stor ulempe er transporten. For folk, der bor langt væk fra de store byer, bliver der meget længere til hospitalet. Det kan være farligt i en nødsituation, hvor det skal gå hurtigt, og det er svært for familien at komme på besøg.
Subject: [Compulsory. Fewer but larger hospitals in Denmark] There are becoming fewer but larger hospitals in Denmark. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that the large hospitals have better equipment and more expert help. When the doctors are gathered in one place, they become more specialized, and then they can provide better treatment for seriously ill people. 

However, a major disadvantage is the transportation. For people who live far away from the big cities, it becomes a much longer distance to the hospital. This can be dangerous in an emergency situation where things need to move fast, and it is difficult for the family to visit.

Emne: [Opf.]Mener du, det er vigtigere, at et hospital kan tilbyde den bedst mulige behandling, end at hospitalet ligger tæt på borgerne? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg mener, at den bedst mulige behandling er det vigtigste. Hvis man er meget alvorligt syg, for eksempel med kræft eller en hjertesygdom, har man brug for eksperter og det bedste udstyr for at overleve. Det betyder mindre, at man skal køre i længere tid for at komme dertil, hvis bare behandlingen er god. 

Men på den anden side skal der stadig være akutbiler og små lægevagter tæt på borgerne, så man kan få hurtig hjælp, hvis det er en akut nødsituation.
Subject: [Follow-up. Fewer but larger hospitals in Denmark] Do you think it is more important that a hospital can offer the best possible treatment than for the hospital to be close to the citizens? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I believe that the best possible treatment is the most important thing. If you are very seriously ill, for example with cancer or a heart disease, you need experts and the best equipment to survive. It matters less that you have to drive for a longer time to get there, as long as the treatment is good. 

But on the other hand, there should still be emergency vehicles and small medical clinics close to the citizens so that you can get quick help if it is an acute emergency.

Emne: [Obl.]Der er nogle, der mener, at der skal være mere og billigere offentlig transport. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det for samfundet?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er for miljøet og klimaet. Hvis busser og tog bliver billigere, vil mange mennesker lade bilen stå derhjemme og rejse sammen i stedet for. Det betyder mindre CO2 og renere luft i byerne. 

Desuden hjælper det folk med færre penge, som for eksempel studerende og pensionister, fordi de nemmere kan komme rundt. 

Men en stor ulempe er økonomien. Det koster rigtig mange penge for staten at lave mere offentlig transport og gøre billetterne billige. De penge skal findes et andet sted, for eksempel ved at betale mere i skat eller spare på sygehusene.
Subject: [Compulsory. More and cheaper public transportation] There are some who believe that there should be more and cheaper public transportation. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this for society?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is for the environment and the climate. If buses and trains become cheaper, many people will leave the car at home and travel together instead. This means less CO2 and cleaner air in the cities. 

Furthermore, it helps people with less money, such as students and pensioners, because they can get around more easily. 

However, a major disadvantage is the economy. It costs a really lot of money for the state to create more public transportation and make the tickets cheap. That money has to be found somewhere else, for example by paying more in taxes or cutting back on hospitals.

Emne: [Opf.]Hvad tror du, det vil betyde for miljøet, hvis den offentlige transport bliver billigere?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det vil betyde meget for miljøet. Hvis billetterne bliver billigere, vil flere mennesker vælge at tage bussen eller toget i stedet for bilen. 

Det betyder, at der kommer færre biler på vejene, og så vil der være mindre forurening og mindre CO2 i luften. Det vil give os renere luft i byerne, og det er godt for klimaet. 

Men det virker kun, hvis togene og busserne også kommer til tiden, så folk har lyst til at bruge dem.
Subject: [Follow-up. More and cheaper public transportation] What do you think it will mean for the environment if public transportation becomes cheaper?
Presentation:
I think it will mean a lot for the environment. If tickets become cheaper, more people will choose to take the bus or the train instead of the car. 

This means there will be fewer cars on the roads, and then there will be less pollution and less CO2 in the air. This will give us cleaner air in the cities, and that is good for the climate. 

But it only works if the trains and buses also arrive on time, so people want to use them.

Emne: [B-obl. Mænd i traditttionelle kvindejob]I Danmark er der færre mænd end kvinder, der arbejder med børn, fx som lærer eller pædagog. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om gamle traditioner. I mange år har folk tænkt, at det er kvinder, som er bedst til at passe og pleje små børn. Derfor vælger mange mænd ikke disse uddannelser, fordi de tror, det er et kvindejob. 

For det andet handler det om lønnen. Job som pædagog eller lærer har tit en lavere løn end job i mandedominerede brancher som IT eller byggeri. Mange mænd vil gerne tjene flere penge og vælger derfor noget andet.
Subject: In Denmark, there are fewer men than women working with children, for example, as a teacher or pedagogue. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about old traditions. For many years, people have thought that women are the best at taking care of and nurturing small children. Therefore, many men do not choose these educations because they think it is a woman's job. 

Secondly, it is about the salary. Jobs as a pedagogue or teacher often have a lower salary than jobs in male-dominated industries like IT or construction. Many men want to earn more money and therefore choose something else.

Emne: [B-opf. Mænd i traditttionelle kvindejob]Hvad tror du, det betyder for børnene, at der er færre mænd end kvinder, der er pædagoger eller lærere?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det betyder, at børnene mangler mandlige rollemodeller i hverdagen. Børn har brug for at se og snakke med både mænd og kvinder for at lære om verden. 

Desuden leger mænd og kvinder ofte på forskellige måder. Mænd kan måske godt lide at lave mere vild sport, bygge ting eller spille computer med børnene, og det går børnene glip af, når der næsten kun er kvinder. 

Det er bedst for børnene at have en god blanding i skolen og børnehaven.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Men in traditional women's jobs] What do you think it means for the children that there are fewer men than women who are pedagogues or teachers?
Presentation:
I think it means that the children lack male role models in their daily lives. Children need to see and talk to both men and women to learn about the world. 

Furthermore, men and women often play in different ways. Men might like to do more wild sports, build things, or play computer games with the children, and the children miss out on that when there are almost only women. 

It is best for the children to have a good mix in school and kindergarten.

Emne: [Obl.]Man kunne lave regler for, hvor mange kvinder og mænd der kan optages på forskellige uddannelser, så der ikke er nogen uddannelser, hvor der næsten kun er kvinder eller kun mænd. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der ville være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at det kan skabe mere ligestilling i samfundet. Hvis der er en god blanding af mænd og kvinder på studierne, lærer de at samarbejde bedre, og det vil også skabe et bedre arbejdsmiljø bagefter. 

Men en stor ulempe er, at det kan være unfair for de unge. Hvis en mand er super dygtig og har gode karakterer, men ikke kan komme ind på IT-studiet, fordi pladserne til mænd er brugt op, er det ikke retfærdigt. Man skal optages på grund af sine evner, ikke sit køn.
Subject: [Compulsory. Women and men in education] One could make rules for how many women and men can be admitted to different study programs, so that there are no study programs where there are almost only women or only men. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there would be to this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that it can create more equality in society. If there is a good mix of men and women in the studies, they learn to cooperate better, and it will also create a better working environment afterwards. 

However, a major disadvantage is that it can be unfair to young people. If a man is super talented and has good grades, but cannot get into the IT study because the spots for men are filled up, it is not fair. You should be admitted because of your abilities, not your gender.

Emne: [Opf.]Synes du, det er vigtigt med en mere lige fordeling af kvinder og mænd i de forskellige brancher på arbejdsmarkedet? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, det synes jeg er meget vigtigt. 

For det første handler det om retfærdighed. Mænd og kvinder skal have de samme muligheder for at få gode job og tjene penge. 

For det andet tror jeg, at arbejdspladserne bliver meget bedre, hvis der er både mænd og kvinder. De tænker tit forskelligt og har forskellige gode idéer, og det gør, at man kan løse problemerne bedre. Hvis der kun er ét køn i en branche, går virksomheden glip af mange gode talenter.
Subject: [Follow-up. Women and men in education] Do you think it is important to have a more equal distribution of women and men in the different sectors of the labor market? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think that is very important. 

Firstly, it is about fairness. Men and women should have the same opportunities to get good jobs and earn money. 

Secondly, I believe that workplaces become much better if there are both men and women. They often think differently and have different good ideas, and that makes it easier to solve problems well. If there is only one gender in an industry, the company misses out on a lot of great talent.

Emne: [Obl.]Flere kommuner bruger penge på dyre kulturhuse med kulturtilbud som fx teater, dans, musik og udstillinger. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at det samler mennesker og skaber et godt fællesskab i byen. Kulturhuse giver folk mulighed for at opleve spændende kunst, musik og teater tæt på deres hjem, og det gør kommunen til et sjovere sted at bo. 

Men en stor ulempe er økonomien. Det koster rigtig mange penge at bygge og passe de store huse. Hvis kommunen bruger for mange penge på kultur, har de måske færre penge til vigtige ting som folkeskoler, ældrepleje eller bedre veje.
Subject: [Compulsory. Expensive culture houses in municipalities] Several municipalities spend money on expensive culture houses with cultural offerings such as theater, dance, music, and exhibitions. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that it brings people together and creates a good community in the city. Culture houses give people the opportunity to experience exciting art, music, and theater close to their homes, and that makes the municipality a more fun place to live. 

However, a major disadvantage is the economy. It costs a really lot of money to build and maintain the large buildings. If the municipality spends too much money on culture, they might have less money for important things like public schools, elderly care, or better roads.

Emne: [Opf.]Mener du, at en kommune skal bruge penge på kulturhuse, eller bør en kommune prioritere andre kulturtilbud eller andre områder som fx ældrepleje og uddannelse? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Jeg synes, at kommunerne skal passe på med at bruge for mange penge på store kulturhuse. 

Det er selvfølgelig godt med kultur, men områder som ældrepleje og uddannelse er meget vigtigere for borgernes hverdag. Hvis de ældre ikke får en god pleje, eller hvis skolerne mangler lærere og bøger, så fungerer samfundet ikke godt. 

Derfor mener jeg, at kommunen først skal bruge penge på velfærd, og hvis der er penge i overskud, kan man bruge dem på kultur.
Subject: [Follow-up. Expensive culture houses in municipalities] Do you believe that a municipality should spend money on culture houses, or should a municipality prioritize other cultural offerings or other areas such as elderly care and education? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
I think that the municipalities should be careful about spending too much money on large culture houses. 

It is of course good to have culture, but areas like elderly care and education are much more important for the citizens' daily lives. If the elderly do not get good care, or if the schools lack teachers and books, then society does not function well. 

Therefore, I believe that the municipality should first spend money on welfare, and if there is money left over, it can be spent on culture.

Emne: [A-obl. Køb og salg via sociale medier]Flere og flere køber og sælger produkter via sociale medier. Hvorfor tror du, de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første er det meget nemt og hurtigt. Man kan sidde i sin sofa og kigge på ting på sin telefon, og man kan hurtigt skrive en besked til sælgeren. 

For det andet er det ofte billigere end at købe nye ting i en rigtig butik. Mange mennesker vil gerne spare penge, og samtidig er det godt for miljøet, når vi genbruger ting i stedet for at smide dem ud.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Buying and selling via social media] More and more people buy and sell products via social media. Why do you think they do that?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is very easy and fast. You can sit on your couch and look at things on your phone, and you can quickly write a message to the seller. 

Secondly, it is often cheaper than buying new things in a real shop. Many people want to save money, and at the same time, it is good for the environment when we reuse things instead of throwing them away.

Emne: [A-opf. Køb og salg via sociale medier]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved at købe og sælge produkter via sociale medier? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg tror, der kan være to store problemer. 

Det første problem er snyd. Man kan risikere at betale for en ting, som man aldrig modtager, fordi sælgeren forsvinder med pengene. 

Det andet problem er kvaliteten. Når man køber noget online, kan man ikke røre ved tingen eller prøve den først. Derfor kan man blive skuffet, hvis varen er i stykker eller ser anderledes ud i virkeligheden end på billedet.
Subject: [A-follow-up. Buying and selling via social media] Do you think there can be any problems with buying and selling products via social media? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be two big problems. 

The first problem is scams. You can risk paying for a thing that you never receive because the seller disappears with the money. 

The second problem is the quality. When you buy something online, you cannot touch the thing or try it first. Therefore, you can be disappointed if the item is broken or looks different in reality than in the picture.

Emne: [B-obl. Personlige billeder på sociale medier]Mange mennesker lægger private og personlige billeder ud på de sociale medier. Hvorfor tror du, de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om at dele sit liv med venner og familie. Det er en nem og hurtig måde at vise sine ferier, fester eller hverdagen til de mennesker, man holder af, især hvis de bor langt væk. 

For det andet tror jeg, at folk godt kan lide at få opmærksomhed. Når man lægger et flot billede op, får man "likes" og positive kommentarer, og det gør de fleste mennesker glade og stolte.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Personal pictures on social media] Many people post private and personal pictures on social media. Why do you think they do that?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about sharing one's life with friends and family. It is an easy and fast way to show your holidays, parties, or daily life to the people you care about, especially if they live far away. 

Secondly, I think that people like to get attention. When you post a nice picture, you get "likes" and positive comments, and that makes most people happy and proud.

Emne: [B-opf. Personlige billeder på sociale medier]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved at lægge private og personlige billeder ud på de sociale medier? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg mener, der kan være to store problemer med det. 

For det første mister man kontrollen over sine billeder. Når et billede først ligger på internettet, kan andre mennesker gemme det eller dele det uden tilladelse, og det kan være ubehageligt. 

For det andet kan det skabe et forkert billede af virkeligheden. Mange viser kun de perfekte øjeblikke, og det kan gøre andre mennesker misundelige eller kede af det, fordi de føler, at deres eget liv er kedeligt.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Personal pictures on social media] Do you think there can be any problems with posting private and personal pictures on social media? Why?/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be two big problems with that. 

Firstly, you lose control of your pictures. Once a picture is on the internet, other people can save it or share it without permission, and that can be uncomfortable. 

Secondly, it can create a false picture of reality. Many people only show the perfect moments, and that can make other people envious or sad because they feel that their own life is boring.

Emne: [Obl.]I dag læser mange deres nyheder på de sociale medier. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at det er utrolig hurtigt og nemt. Man kan læse nyheder på sin telefon hele tiden, og det koster ikke noget. Man kan også nemt dele nyheder med sine venner og diskutere dem. 

Men en stor ulempe er, at der er mange falske nyheder på sociale medier. Det er svært at vide, om historierne er sande, fordi alle kan skrive hvad som helst. 

Desuden læser man ofte kun de nyheder, som man er enig i, og det er ikke så godt for demokratiet.
Subject: [Compulsory. News on social media] Today, many people read their news on social media. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that it is incredibly fast and easy. You can read news on your phone all the time, and it does not cost anything. You can also easily share news with your friends and discuss them. 

However, a major disadvantage is that there is a lot of fake news on social media. It is difficult to know if the stories are true because anyone can write anything. 

Furthermore, you often only read the news that you agree with, and that is not very good for democracy.

Emne: [Opf.]Mener du, at de sociale medier har en positiv eller negativ betydning for demokratiet?
Praesentation:
Jeg mener, at sociale medier har både en positiv og en negativ betydning. 

Det positive er, at det er nemt for alle mennesker at sige deres mening og deltage i debatten. Politikere kan også tale direkte til borgerne. 

Det negative er dog, at der er mange falske nyheder, som kan snyde folk. Derudover taler folk ofte grimt til hinanden i kommentarerne, og det kan gøre, at nogle ikke har lyst til at deltage i demokratiet.
Subject: [Follow-up. News on social media] Do you believe that social media has a positive or negative impact on democracy?
Presentation:
I believe that social media has both a positive and a negative impact. 

The positive part is that it is easy for all people to express their opinion and participate in the debate. Politicians can also speak directly to the citizens. 

However, the negative part is that there is a lot of fake news that can trick people. In addition, people often speak rudely to each other in the comments, and that can make some people not want to participate in democracy.

Emne: [A-obl. Firmafest]På mange arbejdspladser har man sociale arrangementer som fx firmafester og skovture. Hvorfor tror du, man har det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om det gode arbejdsmiljø. Når kolleger mødes til en fest, taler de ikke kun om arbejde, men lærer hinanden bedre at kende som mennesker. Det gør samarbejdet i hverdagen meget nemmere og sjovere. 

For det andet giver det medarbejderne mere motivation. Folk bliver glade for deres arbejdsplads, når chefen arrangerer noget hyggeligt for dem, og så har de lyst til at arbejde der i lang tid.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Company party] At many workplaces, they have social events such as company parties and picnics. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about a good working environment. When colleagues meet at a party, they do not only talk about work, but get to know each other better as people. This makes cooperation in daily life much easier and more fun. 

Secondly, it gives the employees more motivation. People become happy with their workplace when the boss arranges something cozy for them, and then they want to work there for a long time.

Emne: [A-opf. Firmafest]Hvad tror du, det betyder for medarbejdernes tilfredshed med deres arbejde, at man har sociale arrangementer?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det betyder rigtig meget for medarbejdernes tilfredshed. Når man har sociale arrangementer, bliver man gladere for at gå på arbejde, fordi man har gode minder med sine kolleger. 

Det gør også, at man føler sig som en del af et hold, og ikke bare som en maskine, der arbejder. Hvis man har det sjovt sammen i fritiden, accepterer man også nemmere stressede perioder på jobbet, fordi arbejdsmiljøet generelt er godt.
Subject: [A-follow-up. Company party] What do you think it means for employees' job satisfaction that they have social events?
Presentation:
I think it means a very great deal for employee satisfaction. When you have social events, you become happier to go to work because you have good memories with your colleagues. 

It also makes you feel like part of a team, and not just like a machine that works. If you have fun together in your free time, you also accept stressful periods at work more easily because the working environment is generally good.

Emne: [B-obl. Kursus i teambuilding]På mange arbejdspladser kommer medarbejderne på kursus i teambuilding. Hvorfor tror du, de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om at forbedre samarbejdet. Når kolleger laver øvelser og løser opgaver sammen uden for kontoret, lærer de at stole på hinanden. Det gør, at de arbejder bedre sammen i hverdagen. 

For det andet handler det om kommunikation. På et teambuilding-kursus lærer man at tale bedre sammen og løse konflikter på en god måde. Det skaber en bedre stemning på arbejdspladsen.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Course in teambuilding] At many workplaces, employees go on a course in teambuilding. Why do you think they do that?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about improving cooperation. When colleagues do exercises and solve tasks together outside the office, they learn to trust each other. This makes them work better together in daily life. 

Secondly, it is about communication. On a teambuilding course, you learn to talk better together and solve conflicts in a good way. This creates a better atmosphere at the workplace.

Emne: [B-opf. Kursus i teambuilding]Hvad tror du, det betyder for medarbejdernes tilfredshed med deres arbejde, at de kommer på kursus i teambuilding?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan have en stor betydning for medarbejdernes tilfredshed. 

Når man tager afsted sammen på kursus, lærer man sine kolleger at kende på en ny måde, og det gør hverdagen hyggeligere bagefter. Man føler sig mere som en del af et team, og det giver god energi. 

Men hvis medarbejderne har for travlt med deres rigtige arbejde, kan det også have den modsatte effekt. Så bliver de måske stressede, fordi de føler, at de spilder vigtig tid, som de skulle bruge på deres opgaver.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Course in teambuilding] What do you think it means for employees' job satisfaction that they go on a course in teambuilding?
Presentation:
I think it can have a great significance for employee satisfaction. 

When you go away together on a course, you get to know your colleagues in a new way, and that makes daily life cozier afterwards. You feel more like part of a team, and that gives good energy. 

However, if the employees are too busy with their real work, it can also have the opposite effect. Then they might get stressed because they feel that they are wasting important time that they should spend on their tasks.

Emne: [Obl.]I nogle virksomheder giver man en bonus – altså en ekstra sum penge – til medarbejdere, hvis de gør en særlig indsats for virksomheden. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at det motiverer medarbejderne til at arbejde hårdere. Når man ved, at man kan tjene ekstra penge, gør man sig mere umage, og det er godt for virksomhedens succes. 

Men en stor ulempe er, at det kan skabe dårlig stemning og jalousi på arbejdspladsen. Hvis kun nogle få medarbejdere får en bonus, kan de andre blive kede af det eller vrede. Det kan ødelægge samarbejdet, fordi folk begynder at konkurrere mod hinanden i stedet for at hjælpe hinanden som et team.
Subject: [Compulsory. Bonus for employees] In some companies, a bonus – that is, an extra sum of money – is given to employees if they make a special effort for the company. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that it motivates employees to work harder. When you know that you can earn extra money, you do your best, and that is good for the company's success. 

However, a major disadvantage is that it can create a bad atmosphere and jealousy at the workplace. If only a few employees get a bonus, the others might become sad or angry. It can ruin cooperation because people start competing against each other instead of helping each other as a team.

Emne: [Opf.]Hvordan tror du, en bonus påvirker samarbejdet mellem medarbejderne? Og mellem medarbejderne og ledelsen?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at en bonus påvirker samarbejdet mellem medarbejderne negativt. 

Når folk kæmper om penge, stopper de med at hjælpe hinanden, fordi de hellere vil vinde selv. Det ødelægger det gode team. 

Mellem medarbejderne og ledelsen kan det også skabe problemer. Hvis medarbejderne føler, at chefen giver bonussen uretfærdigt, mister de tilliden til ledelsen. Det kan gøre, at folk bliver utilfredse og ikke har lyst til at lytte til chefen.
Subject: [Follow-up. Bonus for employees] How do you think a bonus affects the cooperation between employees? And between the employees and the management?
Presentation:
I think that a bonus affects the cooperation between employees negatively. 

When people fight for money, they stop helping each other because they would rather win themselves. It ruins the good team. 

Between the employees and the management, it can also create problems. If the employees feel that the boss gives the bonus unfairly, they lose trust in the management. This can make people dissatisfied and not want to listen to the boss.

Emne: [A-obl. Jobsøgning som nyuddannet]Nogle nyuddannede kan have svært ved at finde det første job. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første mangler nyuddannede ofte erhvervserfaring. Mange virksomheder vil gerne have medarbejdere, der allerede ved, hvordan man arbejder, og som kan starte med det samme uden meget hjælp. 

For det andet er der stor konkurrence om de gode job. Der er mange unge, som er færdige med skolen på samme tid, og som søger de samme stillinger. Derfor kan det tage lang tid at finde noget.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Job search as a recent graduate] Some recent graduates can find it difficult to find their first job. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, recent graduates often lack work experience. Many companies want employees who already know how to work and who can start right away without much help. 

Secondly, there is great competition for the good jobs. There are many young people who finish school at the same time and who apply for the same positions. Therefore, it can take a long time to find something.

Emne: [A-opf. Jobsøgning som nyuddannet]Hvad tror du, erfaring fra fx et studiejob eller praktik kan betyde for de nyuddannedes muligheder for at finde et job?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at erfaring fra et studiejob eller praktik betyder rigtig meget. 

For det første lærer man, hvordan en rigtig arbejdsplads fungerer, og man kan bruge sin viden fra studiet i praksis. Det gør, at chefen føler sig mere tryg, når han skal ansætte en nyuddannet. 

For det andet kan man møde mange vigtige mennesker i branchen. Hvis man gør et godt stykke arbejde i sin praktik, kan det være, at virksomheden tilbyder et rigtige job bagefter.
Subject: [A-follow-up. Job search as a recent graduate] What do you think experience from, for example, a student job or internship can mean for recent graduates' chances of finding a job?
Presentation:
I think that experience from a student job or internship means a very great deal. 

Firstly, you learn how a real workplace functions, and you can use your knowledge from your studies in practice. This makes the boss feel more secure when hiring a recent graduate. 

Secondly, you can meet many important people in the industry. If you do a good job during your internship, it might be that the company offers you a real job afterwards.

Emne: [B-obl. Ældre Jobsøgende]Jobsøgende over 55 år kan have svært ved at finde et nyt job, selvom de har mange års erfaring. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første tænker nogle arbejdsgivere på fremtiden. De vil hellere ansætte en ung person, som kan blive i virksomheden i mange år, i stedet for en ældre, som måske går på pension om ti år. 

For det andet tror jeg, at nogle chefer er bange for, at ældre medarbejdere er svære at lære nye ting, for eksempel nye computersystemer. De tror måske også, at ældre medarbejdere vil have en meget høj løn, fordi de har meget erfaring.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Older Job Seekers] Job seekers over 55 can find it difficult to find a new job, even though they have many years of experience. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, some employers think about the future. They prefer to hire a young person who can stay in the company for many years, instead of an older person who might retire in ten years. 

Secondly, I think that some bosses are afraid that older employees are difficult to teach new things, for example, new computer systems. They might also think that older employees want a very high salary because they have a lot of experience.

Emne: [B-opf. Ældre Jobsøgende]Mener du, der kan være nogen problemer ved, at nogle arbejdsgivere fravælger jobsøgende over 55 år med mange års erfaring? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, jeg mener, der kan være to store problemer ved det. 

For det første mister virksomhederne en masse vigtig viden. Ældre medarbejdere har prøvet mange ting i deres arbejdsliv, så de er gode til at løse svære opgaver og bevare roen i stressede situationer. 

For det andet er det et problem for samfundet. Regeringen vil gerne have, at vi arbejder i flere år, men hvis folk over 55 år ikke kan få et job, bliver det svært. Det kan gøre de ældre triste og frustrerede, fordi de føler, at samfundet ikke kan bruge dem.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Older Job Seekers] Do you think there can be any problems with some employers deselecting job seekers over 55 with many years of experience? Why/Why not?
Presentation:
Yes, I think there can be two major problems with that. 

Firstly, companies lose a lot of important knowledge. Older employees have tried many things in their working lives, so they are good at solving difficult tasks and staying calm in stressful situations. 

Secondly, it is a problem for society. The government wants us to work for more years, but if people over 55 cannot get a job, it becomes difficult. It can make older people sad and frustrated because they feel that society has no use for them.

Emne: [Obl.]De fleste jobsøgende i Danmark kan modtage økonomisk hjælp som fx dagpenge eller kontanthjælp, mens de søger job. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det for samfundet?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at det giver folk tryghed. Når man mister sit job, kan man stadig betale sin husleje og købe mad til sin familie. Det betyder, at folk ikke ender i fattigdom, og det er godt for samfundet. 

Men en stor ulempe kan være økonomien for staten. Det koster rigtig mange penge i skat at hjælpe så mange mennesker. Derudover er der nogle, som måske bliver dovne og ikke søger job så hurtigt, fordi de får penge uden at arbejde.
Subject: [Compulsory. Financial aid for job seekers] Most job seekers in Denmark can receive financial help such as unemployment benefits or social assistance while they are looking for a job. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this for society?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that it gives people security. When you lose your job, you can still pay your rent and buy food for your family. This means that people do not end up in poverty, and that is good for society. 

However, a major disadvantage can be the economy for the state. It costs a really lot of money in taxes to help so many people. In addition, there are some who might become lazy and not look for a job as quickly because they get money without working.

Emne: [Opf.]Hvad tror du, den økonomiske hjælp kan betyde for de arbejdsløses jobsøgning?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den økonomiske hjælp har to forskellige betydninger for jobsøgningen. 

På den ene side giver pengene de arbejdsløse ro og mindre stress. Når man ikke skal være bange for at miste sit hus, har man overskud til at søge de rigtige job, hvor man kan bruge sine kompetencer bedst. 

På den anden side kan det dog også betyde, at nogle personer venter for længe med at søge job. Hvis hjælpen er høj, har de måske ikke så travlt med at finde et nyt arbejde, og det kan gøre det sværere at komme tilbage på arbejdsmarkedet bagefter.
Subject: [Follow-up. Financial aid for job seekers] What do you think the financial aid can mean for the unemployed people's job search?
Presentation:
I think that the financial aid has two different meanings for the job search. 

On one hand, the money gives the unemployed peace of mind and less stress. When you do not have to be afraid of losing your house, you have the energy to look for the right jobs where you can use your skills best. 

On the other hand, however, it can also mean that some people wait too long to look for a job. If the aid is high, they might not be in such a hurry to find a new job, and that can make it harder to come back to the job market afterwards.

Emne: [A-obl. Forældre med frivilligt arbejde]Mange af dem, der arbejder frivilligt, er forældre med hjemmeboende børn. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om deres børn. Mange forældre vil gerne hjælpe til i børnenes fritidsklubber, sportsklubber eller skoler, for eksempel som fodboldtræner. De gør det for at støtte deres egne børn og være sammen med dem. 

For det andet handler det om fællesskab. Når forældre arbejder frivilligt, lærer de andre forældre i lokalområdet at kende. Det giver et godt netværk, og man kan hjælpe hinanden i hverdagen.
Subject: [A-compulsory. Parents with voluntary work] Many of those who volunteer are parents with children living at home. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about their children. Many parents want to help out in their children's leisure clubs, sports clubs, or schools, for example as a football coach. They do it to support their own children and spend time with them. 

Secondly, it is about community. When parents volunteer, they get to know other parents in the local area. It provides a good network, and you can help each other in daily life.

Emne: [A-opf. Forældre med frivilligt arbejde]Hvad tror du, det betyder for familielivet, at mange af dem, der arbejder frivilligt, er forældre med hjemmeboende børn?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan betyde to forskellige ting for familielivet. 

På den ene side er det meget positivt, fordi forældre og børn laver noget aktivt sammen i deres fritid. Det giver gode oplevelser og minder, og børnene bliver glade for, at forældrene viser interesse for deres hobbyer. 

På den anden side kan det også skabe stress. Hvis forældrene bruger for meget tid på frivilligt arbejde, har de måske mindre tid og energi derhjemme til at slappe af, lave mad eller hjælpe med lektier.
Subject: [A-follow-up. Parents with voluntary work] What do you think it means for family life that many of those who volunteer are parents with children living at home?
Presentation:
I think it can mean two different things for family life. 

On one hand, it is very positive because parents and children do something active together in their free time. It creates good experiences and memories, and the children become happy that their parents show an interest in their hobbies. 

On the other hand, it can also create stress. If parents spend too much time on voluntary work, they might have less time and energy at home to relax, cook, or help with homework.

Emne: [B-obl. Pensionister med frivilligt arbejde]Mange af dem, der arbejder frivilligt er pensionister. Hvorfor tror du, det er sådan?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, der er to vigtige grunde til det. 

For det første handler det om tid. Pensionister arbejder ikke mere, og derfor har de masser af frihed i hverdagen til at hjælpe andre. 

For det andet handler det om det sociale liv. Når man stopper med at gå på arbejde, kan man hurtigt komme til at føle sig ensom derhjemme. Frivilligt arbejde er en god måde at møde nye mennesker på, få nye venner og føle, at man stadig gør en vigtig forskel i samfundet.
Subject: [B-compulsory. Pensioners with voluntary work] Many of those who volunteer are pensioners. Why do you think that is?
Presentation:
I think there are two important reasons for that. 

Firstly, it is about time. Pensioners do not work anymore, and therefore they have lots of freedom in their daily life to help others. 

Secondly, it is about social life. When you stop going to work, you can easily start to feel lonely at home. Voluntary work is a good way to meet new people, get new friends, and feel that you are still making an important difference in society.

Emne: [B-opf. Pensionister med frivilligt arbejde]Hvad tror du, det betyder for pensionisters livskvalitet, at de arbejder frivilligt?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det betyder rigtig meget for deres livskvalitet. 

For det første giver det dem en grund til at stå op om morgenen, fordi de føler, at andre har brug for dem. Det gør dem glade og stolte. 

For det andet holder det dem aktive, både i kroppen og i hjernen, fordi de skal løse opgaver og tale med mange forskellige mennesker. Når man er aktiv og har et formål, tror jeg, man lever et længere og gladere liv som pensionist.
Subject: [B-follow-up. Pensioners with voluntary work] What do you think it means for pensioners' quality of life that they volunteer?
Presentation:
I think it means a very great deal for their quality of life. 

Firstly, it gives them a reason to get up in the morning because they feel that others need them. This makes them happy and proud. 

Secondly, it keeps them active, both in body and in brain, because they have to solve tasks and talk to many different people. When you are active and have a purpose, I believe you live a longer and happier life as a pensioner.

Emne: [Obl.]Kommunerne gør mere og mere brug af frivillig arbejdskraft inden for fx ældrepleje og sundhed. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at de ældre og syge kan få mere omsorg og selskab. De frivillige har god tid til at snakke, gå ture eller drikke kaffe med dem, og det mindsker ensomhed. Det sparer også kommunen for mange penge. 

Men en stor ulempe er, at de frivillige ikke har en rigtig uddannelse som sygeplejersker eller SOSU-assistenter. De kender ikke reglerne for medicin eller professionel pleje, og det kan være farligt for sikkerheden. 

Desuden er det unfair, hvis kommunen bruger frivillige til at spare rigtige job væk.
Subject: [Compulsory. Voluntary labor in municipalities] Municipalities are making more and more use of voluntary labor within, for example, elderly care and health. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there can be to this?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that the elderly and sick can get more care and company. The volunteers have plenty of time to talk, go for walks, or drink coffee with them, and that reduces loneliness. It also saves the municipality a lot of money. 

However, a major disadvantage is that the volunteers do not have a real education as nurses or social and health care assistants. They do not know the rules for medicine or professional care, and that can be dangerous for safety. 

Furthermore, it is unfair if the municipality uses volunteers to cut away real jobs.

Emne: [Opf.]Hvad mener du om, at kommuner gør brug af frivillig arbejdskraft for at spare penge på løn til professionelle?
Praesentation: Jeg synes, det er en dårlig idé, hvis kommunerne kun gør det for at spare penge. Det skal være de professionelle, som laver det vigtige arbejde, fordi de har den rigtige uddannelse og ved, hvad de gør. De frivillige skal kun være et ekstra tilbud, som kan hygge om de ældre. Hvis man sparer de rigtige job væk, mister vi kvalitet i plejen, og det er ikke fair over for de professionelle, som mangler arbejde.
Subject: [Follow-up. Voluntary labor in municipalities] What do you think about municipalities using voluntary labor to save money on salaries for professionals?
Presentation: I think it is a bad idea if the municipalities only do it to save money. It should be the professionals who do the important work because they have the right education and know what they are doing. The volunteers should only be an extra offer to cozy up with the elderly. If real jobs are cut away, we lose quality in the care, and it is not fair to the professionals who need work.

Emne: [A1]Nogle mennesker vælger i perioder at arbejde på deltid. Hvorfor tror du, de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at mange mennesker vælger deltid, fordi de gerne vil have mere balance i deres liv. For eksempel har børnefamilier ofte meget travlt i hverdagen. Hvis en af forældrene arbejder på deltid, er der mere tid til at hente børn, lave mad og slappe af. 

Andre vælger det, fordi de har stress, eller fordi de vil have mere tid til deres hobbyer. Det handler om at få mere frihed, selvom man tjener færre penge.
Subject: [Working life] Some people choose to work part-time for periods. Why do you think they do that?
Presentation:
I think that many people choose part-time because they want more balance in their life. For example, families with children are often very busy in their daily lives. If one of the parents works part-time, there is more time to pick up the children, cook, and relax. 

Others choose it because they have stress, or because they want more time for their hobbies. It is about getting more freedom, even though you earn less money.

Emne: [A2]Hvad tror du, det betyder for folks karrieremuligheder, at de i perioder arbejder på deltid?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan have både gode og dårlige konsekvenser. 

På den ene side kan det skade karrieren, fordi man ikke har så meget tid til store projekter, og chefen vælger måske en anden til en forfremmelse. 

På den anden side kan deltid give folk mere energi. Hvis man undgår stress, kan man arbejde bedre i det lange løb. Så i en kort periode behøver det ikke at ødelægge ens karriere.
Subject: What do you think it means for people's career opportunities that they work part-time for periods?
Presentation:
I think it can have both good and bad consequences. 

On one hand, it can damage one's career because you do not have as much time for big projects, and the boss might choose someone else for a promotion. 

On the other hand, part-time can give people more energy. If you avoid stress, you can work better in the long run. So for a short period, it does not have to ruin your career.

Emne: [B1]Nogle mennesker tjekker deres arbejdsmails om aftenen eller i weekenden. Hvorfor tror du, de gør det?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at mange gør det, fordi de føler et stort ansvar for deres arbejde. De vil gerne være velforberedte til næste dag, så de ikke bliver stressede om morgenen. 

Desuden er det i dag meget nemt at tjekke mobilen hele tiden. Nogle chefer forventer måske også, at man svarer hurtigt, og det kan skabe et pres, selvom man har fri.
Subject: Some people check their work emails in the evening or during the weekend. Why do you think they do that?
Presentation:
I think that many people do it because they feel a great responsibility for their work. They want to be well-prepared for the next day so they do not get stressed in the morning. 

Furthermore, nowadays it is very easy to check your phone all the time. Some bosses might also expect you to answer quickly, and that can create pressure, even though you are off work.

Emne: [B2]Hvad tror du, det betyder for folks karrieremuligheder, at de tjekker arbejdsmails om aftenen eller i weekenden?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, det kan have to sider. 

På den ene side kan det være godt for karrieren, fordi chefen ser, at man er meget engageret og arbejder hårdt. Det kan måske give en forfremmelse eller en højere løn. 

På den anden side kan det være dårligt på lang sigt. Hvis man aldrig holder fri, bliver man træt og stresset, og så laver man flere fejl. Så det er vigtigt at finde en balance.
Subject: What do you think it means for people's career opportunities that they check work emails in the evening or during the weekend?
Presentation:
I think it can have two sides. 

On one hand, it can be good for one's career because the boss sees that you are very committed and work hard. It might perhaps lead to a promotion or a higher salary. 

On the other hand, it can be bad in the long run. If you never take time off, you get tired and stressed, and then you make more mistakes. So it is important to find a balance.

Emne: [OBL]Nogle mennesker mener, at den almindelige arbejdstid skal sættes ned til 30 timer om ugen. Hvilke fordele og ulemper mener du, der kan være ved et for samfundet?
Praesentation:
Jeg tror, at den største fordel er, at folk vil få mere overskud i hverdagen. Hvis vi arbejder mindre, har vi mere tid til familien, og det kan give mindre stress og færre sygedage. Det er godt for samfundet. 

Men en stor ulempe er, at virksomhederne kan miste penge, fordi produktionen falder. Det kan også blive svært at finde nok medarbejdere i fremtiden, for eksempel på hospitalerne. Så det er en svær balance mellem livskvalitet og økonomi.
Subject: Some people believe that standard working hours should be reduced to 30 hours a week. What advantages and disadvantages do you think there could be for society?
Presentation:
I think that the biggest advantage is that people will have more energy in their daily lives. If we work less, we have more time for family, and that can lead to less stress and fewer sick days. That is good for society. 

However, a major disadvantage is that companies could lose money because production drops. It could also become difficult to find enough employees in the future, for example in hospitals. So it is a difficult balance between quality of life and the economy.

Emne: [OPF]Tror du, at en 30 timers arbejdsuge vil betyde, at arbejdet bliver bedre fordelt mellem folk? Hvorfor?/Hvorfor ikke?
Praesentation:
Ja, det tror jeg. Hvis folk arbejder mindre, for eksempel 30 timer i stedet for 37 timer, så skal firmaerne bruge mere hjælp. Det betyder, at der bliver flere ledige job til dem, som ikke har et arbejde lige nu. På den måde kan vi dele arbejdet bedre.
